<!-- {"role": "tool", "content": "\"\"\"\nAuthentication module using database-backed user management\n\"\"\"\n\nimport hashlib\nimport secrets\nfrom typing import Dict, Set, List, Optional, Tuple\nfrom database import Database\n\n\nclass Authenticator:\n    \"\"\"Database-backed authentication with privilege checking\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, db: Database):\n        self.db = db\n        self.active_sessions: Dict[str, Dict] = {}  # token -> user_info\n    \n    def authenticate(self, username: str, password: str) -> Tuple[bool, Optional[str], Optional[Dict]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Authenticate user against database.\n        \n        Returns:\n            Tuple of (success, session_token, user_info)\n        \"\"\"\n        success, is_admin, privileges = self.db.authenticate_user(username, password)\n        \n        if not success:\n            return False, None, None\n        \n        # Create session\n        token = secrets.token_hex(16)\n        user_info = {\n            \"username\": username,\n            \"is_admin\": is_admin,\n            \"privileges\": privileges\n        }\n        self.active_sessions[token] = user_info\n        \n        return True, token, user_info\n    \n    def validate_session(self, token: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if session token is valid.\"\"\"\n        return token in self.active_sessions\n    \n    def get_user_info(self, token: str) -> Optional[Dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get user info from session token.\"\"\"\n        return self.active_sessions.get(token)\n    \n    def end_session(self, token: str):\n        \"\"\"End a session.\"\"\"\n        if token in self.active_sessions:\n            del self.active_sessions[token]\n    \n    def check_privilege(self, token: str, db_name: str, table_name: str, \n                       required_priv: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if user has required privilege.\"\"\"\n        user_info = self.get_user_info(token)\n        if not user_info:\n            return False\n        \n        username = user_info[\"username\"]\n        is_admin = user_info.get(\"is_admin\", False)\n        \n        # Admins have all privileges\n        if is_admin:\n            return True\n        \n        # Check database privileges\n        return self.db.check_privilege(username, db_name, table_name, required_priv)\n    \n    def has_db_access(self, token: str, db_name: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if user has any access to database.\"\"\"\n        return self.check_privilege(token, db_name, \"*\", \"CONNECT\")\n    \n    def has_table_access(self, token: str, db_name: str, table_name: str, \n                         operation: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if user can perform operation on table.\"\"\"\n        priv_map = {\n            \"SELECT\": \"SELECT\",\n            \"INSERT\": \"INSERT\",\n            \"UPDATE\": \"UPDATE\",\n            \"DELETE\": \"DELETE\",\n            \"CREATE\": \"CREATE\",\n            \"DROP\": \"DROP\"\n        }\n        required_priv = priv_map.get(operation, operation)\n        return self.check_privilege(token, db_name, table_name, required_priv)\n    \n    def is_replication_user(self, token: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if user has REPLICATION SLAVE privilege.\"\"\"\n        user_info = self.get_user_info(token)\n        if not user_info:\n            return False\n        \n        username = user_info[\"username\"]\n        is_admin = user_info.get(\"is_admin\", False)\n        \n        if is_admin:\n            return True\n        \n        # Check for REPLICATION_SLAVE privilege\n        privileges = self.db._get_user_privileges(username)\n        for priv in privileges:\n            if \"REPLICATION_SLAVE\" in priv.get(\"privs\", []):\n                return True\n        \n        return False\n    \n    def create_replication_user(self, username: str, password: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a replication user with REPLICATION SLAVE privilege.\"\"\"\n        # Create the user first\n        result = self.db.create_user(username, password, is_admin=False)\n        if \"already exists\" in result:\n            return result\n        \n        # Grant REPLICATION SLAVE privilege\n        result2 = self.db.grant_privilege(username, \"*\", \"*\", [\"REPLICATION_SLAVE\"])\n        \n        return f\"Replication user '{username}' created with REPLICATION SLAVE privilege\"", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 14, "timestamp": 1783713650.4080472, "date": "2026-07-10", "name": "ReadFile"} -->

import Database\n\n\n# All supported privileges\nALL_PRIVILEGES = {\n    'SELECT', 'INSERT', 'UPDATE', 'DELETE',\n    'CREATE', 'DROP', 'ALTER', 'INDEX',\n    'ADMIN', 'REPLICATION_SLAVE', 'ALL'\n}\n\n\nclass PermissionCache:\n    \"\"\"Thread-safe cache for permission lookups.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, ttl_seconds: int = 300):\n        self._cache: Dict[str, Dict] = {}\n        self._lock = threading.RLock()\n        self._ttl = ttl_seconds\n    \n    def _make_key(self, username: str, db: str, table: str, column: Optional[str] = None) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create cache key.\"\"\"\n        col_part = f\".{column}\" if column else \"\"\n        return f\"{username}:{db}:{table}{col_part}\"\n    \n    def get(self, username: str, db: str, table: str, \n            privilege: str, column: Optional[str] = None) -> Optional[bool]:\n        \"\"\"Get cached permission result.\"\"\"\n        key = self._make_key(username, db, table, column)\n        with self._lock:\n            entry = self._cache.get(key)\n            if entry and entry['expires'] > time.time():\n                return entry['privileges'].get(privilege, False)\n            return None\n    \n    def set(self, username: str, db: str, table: str,\n            privileges: Dict[str, bool], column: Optional[str] = None):\n        \"\"\"Cache permission result.\"\"\"\n        key = self._make_key(username, db, table, column)\n        with self._lock:\n            self._cache[key] = {\n                'privileges': privileges,\n                'expires': time.time() + self._ttl\n            }\n    \n    def invalidate_user(self, username: str):\n        \"\"\"Invalidate all cache entries for a user.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            keys_to_remove = [k for k in self._cache if k.startswith(f\"{username}:\")]\n            for key in keys_to_remove:\n                del self._cache[key]\n    \n    def clear(self):\n        \"\"\"Clear all cache entries.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            self._cache.clear()\n\n\nclass Role:\n    \"\"\"Represents a database role with assigned privileges.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, name: str, description: str = \"\", is_default: bool = False):\n        self.name = name\n        self.description = description\n        self.is_default = is_default\n        self.privileges: Dict[str, Dict[str, Set[str]]] = {}  # db -> table -> set of privs\n    \n    def grant_privilege(self, db: str, table: str, privileges: List[str]):\n        \"\"\"Grant privileges on database/table to role.\"\"\"\n        if db not in self.privileges:\n            self.privileges[db] = {}\n        if table not in self.privileges[db]:\n            self.privileges[db][table] = set()\n        self.privileges[db][table].update(privileges)\n    \n    def revoke_privilege(self, db: str, table: str, privileges: List[str]):\n        \"\"\"Revoke privileges from role.\"\"\"\n        if db in self.privileges and table in self.privileges[db]:\n            self.privileges[db][table].difference_update(privileges)\n            # Clean up empty entries\n            if not self.privileges[db][table]:\n                del self.privileges[db][table]\n            if not self.privileges[db]:\n                del self.privileges[db]\n    \n    def has_privilege(self, db: str, table: str, privilege: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if role has specific privilege.\"\"\"\n        # Check specific table\n        if db in self.privileges:\n            if table in self.privileges[db]:\n                if privilege in self.privileges[db][table] or 'ALL' in self.privileges[db][table]:\n                    return True\n            # Check wildcard table\n            if '*' in self.privileges[db]:\n                if privilege in self.privileges[db]['*'] or 'ALL' in self.privileges[db]['*']:\n                    return True\n        # Check wildcard database\n        if '*' in self.privileges:\n            if table in self.privileges['*']:\n                if privilege in self.privileges['*'][table] or 'ALL' in self.privileges['*'][table]:\n                    return True\n            if '*' in self.privileges['*']:\n                if privilege in self.privileges['*']['*'] or 'ALL' in self.privileges['*']['*']:\n                    return True\n        return False\n    \n    def to_dict(self) -> Dict:\n        \"\"\"Convert to dictionary for storage.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            'name': self.name,\n            'description': self.description,\n            'is_default': self.is_default,\n            'privileges': {\n                db: {\n                    table: list(privs) \n                    for table, privs in tables.items()\n                }\n                for db, tables in self.privileges.items()\n            }\n        }\n    \n    @classmethod\n    def from_dict(cls, data: Dict) -> 'Role':\n        \"\"\"Create from dictionary.\"\"\"\n        role = cls(data['name'], data.get('description', ''), data.get('is_default', False))\n        role.privileges = {\n            db: {\n                table: set(privs)\n                for table, privs in tables.items()\n            }\n            for db, tables in data.get('privileges', {}).items()\n        }\n        return role\n\n\nclass Authenticator:\n    \"\"\"Enhanced database-backed authentication with RBAC.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, db: Database):\n        self.db = db\n        self.active_sessions: Dict[str, Dict] = {}\n        self._sessions_lock = threading.RLock()\n        self.permission_cache = PermissionCache(ttl_seconds=300)\n        self._ensure_system_tables()\n    \n    def _ensure_system_tables(self):\n        \"\"\"Ensure system tables exist for RBAC.\"\"\"\n        # Create roles table\n        self.db._ensure_system_tables()\n        \n        # Ensure roles column exists in users table\n        try:\n            users = self.db._get_table_data('system', 'users')\n            if users and 'roles' not in users[0]:\n                # Migrate existing users\n                for user in users:\n                    user['roles'] = []\n                    user['column_privileges'] = {}\n                self.db._save_table_data('system', 'users', users)\n        except:\n            pass\n    \n    def authenticate(self, username: str, password: str) -> Tuple[bool, Optional[str], Optional[Dict]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Authenticate user against database.\n        \n        Returns:\n            Tuple of (success, session_token, user_info)\n        \"\"\"\n        success, is_admin, privileges = self.db.authenticate_user(username, password)\n        \n        if not success:\n            return False, None, None\n        \n        # Get user roles\n        roles = self._get_user_roles(username)\n        \n        # Create session\n        token = secrets.token_hex(16)\n        user_info = {\n            \"username\": username,\n            \"is_admin\": is_admin,\n            \"privileges\": privileges,\n            \"roles\": roles\n        }\n        \n        with self._sessions_lock:\n            self.active_sessions[token] = user_info\n        \n        return True, token, user_info\n    \n    def validate_session(self, token: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if session token is valid.\"\"\"\n        with self._sessions_lock:\n            return token in self.active_sessions\n    \n    def get_user_info(self, token: str) -> Optional[Dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get user info from session token.\"\"\"\n        with self._sessions_lock:\n            return self.active_sessions.get(token)\n    \n    def end_session(self, token: str):\n        \"\"\"End a session.\"\"\"\n        with self._sessions_lock:\n            if token in self.active_sessions:\n                del self.active_sessions[token]\n    \n    def _get_user_roles(self, username: str) -> List[str]:\n        \"\"\"Get roles assigned to user.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            user = self.db._get_user(username)\n            return user.get('roles', []) if user else []\n        except:\n            return []\n    \n    def _get_role(self, role_name: str) -> Optional[Role]:\n        \"\"\"Get role by name.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            roles_data = self.db._get_table_data('system', 'roles')\n            for role_data in roles_data:\n                if role_data['name'] == role_name:\n                    return Role.from_dict(role_data)\n        except:\n            pass\n        return None\n    \n    def _get_user_effective_privileges(self, username: str, db: str, table: str) -> Set[str]:\n        \"\"\"Get all effective privileges for user (direct + from roles).\"\"\"\n        privileges = set()\n        \n        # Get direct privileges\n        try:\n            user = self.db._get_user(username)\n            if user:\n                user_privs = user.get('privileges', {})\n                if db in user_privs:\n                    if table in user_privs[db]:\n                        privileges.update(user_privs[db][table])\n                    if '*' in user_privs[db]:\n                        privileges.update(user_privs[db]['*'])\n                if '*' in user_privs:\n                    if table in user_privs['*']:\n                        privileges.update(user_privs['*'][table])\n                    if '*' in user_privs['*']:\n                        privileges.update(user_privs['*']['*'])\n        except:\n            pass\n        \n        # Get role privileges\n        roles = self._get_user_roles(username)\n        for role_name in roles:\n            role = self._get_role(role_name)\n            if role:\n                # Check role privileges\n                if role.has_privilege(db, table, 'ALL'):\n                    privileges.update(ALL_PRIVILEGES)\n                else:\n                    for priv in ALL_PRIVILEGES:\n                        if role.has_privilege(db, table, priv):\n                            privileges.add(priv)\n        \n        return privileges\n    \n    def check_privilege(self, token: str, db_name: str, table_name: str, \n                       required_priv: str, column: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if user has required privilege with caching.\n        \n        Supports column-level privileges if column is specified.\n        \"\"\"\n        user_info = self.get_user_info(token)\n        if not user_info:\n            return False\n        \n        username = user_info[\"username\"]\n        is_admin = user_info.get(\"is_admin\", False)\n        \n        # Admins have all privileges\n        if is_admin:\n            return True\n        \n        # Check cache first\n        cached = self.permission_cache.get(username, db_name, table_name, required_priv, column)\n        if cached is not None:\n            return cached\n        \n        # Get effective privileges\n        privileges = self._get_user_effective_privileges(username, db_name, table_name)\n        \n        # Check if has required privilege or ALL\n        has_priv = required_priv in privileges or 'ALL' in privileges\n        \n        # Check column-level privileges if specified\n        if has_priv and column:\n            # Get column-specific privileges\n            try:\n                user = self.db._get_user(username)\n                col_privs = user.get('column_privileges', {})\n                if db_name in col_privs and table_name in col_privs[db_name]:\n                    if column in col_privs[db_name][table_name]:\n                        allowed = col_privs[db_name][table_name][column]\n                        has_priv = required_priv in allowed or 'ALL' in allowed\n            except:\n                pass\n        \n        # Cache result\n        self.permission_cache.set(username, db_name, table_name, {required_priv: has_priv}, column)\n        \n        return has_priv\n    \n    def check_any_privilege(self, token: str, db_name: str, table_name: str,\n                           privileges: List[str]) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if user has any of the required privileges.\"\"\"\n        for priv in privileges:\n            if self.check_privilege(token, db_name, table_name, priv):\n                return True\n        return False\n    \n    def has_db_access(self, token: str, db_name: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if user has any access to database.\"\"\"\n        return self.check_privilege(token, db_name, \"*\", \"CONNECT\") or \\\n               self.check_privilege(token, db_name, \"*\", \"ALL\")\n    \n    def has_table_access(self, token: str, db_name: str, table_name: str, \n                         operation: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if user can perform operation on table.\"\"\"\n        priv_map = {\n            \"SELECT\": \"SELECT\",\n            \"INSERT\": \"INSERT\", \n            \"UPDATE\": \"UPDATE\",\n            \"DELETE\": \"DELETE\",\n            \"CREATE\": \"CREATE\",\n            \"DROP\": \"DROP\",\n            \"ALTER\": \"ALTER\",\n            \"INDEX\": \"INDEX\"\n        }\n        required_priv = priv_map.get(operation, operation)\n        return self.check_privilege(token, db_name, table_name, required_priv)\n    \n    def is_replication_user(self, token: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if user has REPLICATION SLAVE privilege.\"\"\"\n        return self.check_privilege(token, \"*\", \"*\", \"REPLICATION_SLAVE\")\n    \n    # Role Management\n    \n    def create_role(self, role_name: str, description: str = \"\", \n                    is_default: bool = False) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new role.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            roles = self.db._get_table_data('system', 'roles')\n            \n            # Check if exists\n            for role in roles:\n                if role['name'] == role_name:\n                    return f\"ERROR: Role '{role_name}' already exists\"\n            \n            # Create role\n            role = Role(role_name, description, is_default)\n            roles.append(role.to_dict())\n            self.db._save_table_data('system', 'roles', roles)\n            \n            return f\"OK: Role '{role_name}' created\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            return f\"ERROR: Failed to create role: {e}\"\n    \n    def drop_role(self, role_name: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Drop a role.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            roles = self.db._get_table_data('system', 'roles')\n            roles = [r for r in roles if r['name'] != role_name]\n            self.db._save_table_data('system', 'roles', roles)\n            \n            # Remove role from all users\n            users = self.db._get_table_data('system', 'users')\n            for user in users:\n                if 'roles' in user and role_name in user['roles']:\n                    user['roles'].remove(role_name)\n            self.db._save_table_data('system', 'users', users)\n            \n            # Invalidate cache\n            self.permission_cache.clear()\n            \n            return f\"OK: Role '{role_name}' dropped\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            return f\"ERROR: Failed to drop role: {e}\"\n    \n    def grant_role_to_user(self, username: str, role_name: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Assign role to user.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Check role exists\n            role = self._get_role(role_name)\n            if not role:\n                return f\"ERROR: Role '{role_name}' does not exist\"\n            \n            # Update user\n            users = self.db._get_table_data('system', 'users')\n            for user in users:\n                if user['username'] == username:\n                    if 'roles' not in user:\n                        user['roles'] = []\n                    if role_name not in user['roles']:\n                        user['roles'].append(role_name)\n                    break\n            else:\n                return f\"ERROR: User '{username}' not found\"\n            \n            self.db._save_table_data('system', 'users', users)\n            \n            # Invalidate cache\n            self.permission_cache.invalidate_user(username)\n            \n            return f\"OK: Role '{role_name}' granted to '{username}'\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            return f\"ERROR: Failed to grant role: {e}\"\n    \n    def revoke_role_from_user(self, username: str, role_name: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Remove role from user.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            users = self.db._get_table_data('system', 'users')\n            for user in users:\n                if user['username'] == username:\n                    if 'roles' in user and role_name in user['roles']:\n                        user['roles'].remove(role_name)\n                    break\n            else:\n                return f\"ERROR: User '{username}' not found\"\n            \n            self.db._save_table_data('system', 'users', users)\n            \n            # Invalidate cache\n            self.permission_cache.invalidate_user(username)\n            \n            return f\"OK: Role '{role_name}' revoked from '{username}'\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            return f\"ERROR: Failed to revoke role: {e}\"\n    \n    def grant_privilege_to_role(self, role_name: str, db: str, table: str,\n                                 privileges: List[str]) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Grant privileges to a role.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            role = self._get_role(role_name)\n            if not role:\n                return f\"ERROR: Role '{role_name}' does not exist\"\n            \n            # Validate privileges\n            invalid_privs = [p for p in privileges if p not in ALL_PRIVILEGES]\n            if invalid_privs:\n                return f\"ERROR: Invalid privileges: {', '.join(invalid_privs)}\"\n            \n            role.grant_privilege(db, table, privileges)\n            \n            # Save role\n            roles = self.db._get_table_data('system', 'roles')\n            for i, r in enumerate(roles):\n                if r['name'] == role_name:\n                    roles[i] = role.to_dict()\n                    break\n            self.db._save_table_data('system', 'roles', roles)\n            \n            # Invalidate cache for all users with this role\n            self.permission_cache.clear()\n            \n            return f\"OK: Privileges granted to role '{role_name}'\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            return f\"ERROR: Failed to grant privileges to role: {e}\"\n    \n    def show_grants(self, username: Optional[str] = None) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Show grants for user or all users.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            if username:\n                user = self.db._get_user(username)\n                if not user:\n                    return f\"ERROR: User '{username}' not found\"\n                \n                lines = [f\"Grants for {username}:\"]\n                \n                # Direct privileges\n                privileges = user.get('privileges', {})\n                for db, tables in privileges.items():\n                    for table, privs in tables.items():\n                        lines.append(f\"  ON {db}.{table}: {', '.join(privs)}\")\n                \n                # Column privileges\n                col_privs = user.get('column_privileges', {})\n                for db, tables in col_privs.items():\n                    for table, columns in tables.items():\n                        for col, privs in columns.items():\n                            lines.append(f\"  ON {db}.{table}.{col}: {', '.join(privs)}\")\n                \n                # Roles\n                roles = user.get('roles', [])\n                if roles:\n                    lines.append(f\"  ROLES: {', '.join(roles)}\")\n                \n                return '\\n'.join(lines) if len(lines) > 1 else f\"No grants for {username}\"\n            \n            else:\n                # Show all users\n                users = self.db._get_table_data('system', 'users')\n                lines = [\"Grants for all users:\"]\n                for user in users:\n                    user_lines = self.show_grants(user['username']).split('\\n')[1:]\n                    lines.extend(user_lines)\n                    lines.append(\"\")\n                \n                return '\\n'.join(lines)\n                \n        except Exception as e:\n            return f\"ERROR: Failed to show grants: {e}\"\n    \n    def show_roles(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Show all roles.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            roles = self.db._get_table_data('system', 'roles')\n            if not roles:\n                return \"No roles defined\"\n            \n            lines = [\"Roles:\"]\n            for role_data in roles:\n                role = Role.from_dict(role_data)\n                lines.append(f\"  {role.name}: {role.description}\")\n                if role.is_default:\n                    lines[-1] += \" (default)\"\n                \n                for db, tables in role.privileges.items():\n                    for table, privs in tables.items():\n                        lines.append(f\"    ON {db}.{table}: {', '.join(privs)}\")\n            \n            return '\\n'.join(lines)\n        except Exception as e:\n            return f\"ERROR: Failed to show roles: {e}\"\n    \n    def create_replication_user(self, username: str, password: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a replication user with REPLICATION SLAVE privilege.\"\"\"\n        result = self.db.create_user(username, password, is_admin=False)\n        if \"already exists\" in result:\n            return result\n        \n        # Grant REPLICATION SLAVE privilege\n        result2 = self.db.grant_privilege(username, \"*\", \"*\", [\"REPLICATION_SLAVE\"])\n        \n        # Invalidate cache\n        self.permission_cache.invalidate_user(username)\n        \n        return f\"Replication user '{username}' created with REPLICATION SLAVE privilege\"\n    \n    def grant_column_privilege(self, username: str, db: str, table: str,\n                              column: str, privileges: List[str]) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Grant column-level privileges.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            users = self.db._get_table_data('system', 'users')\n            for user in users:\n                if user['username'] == username:\n                    if 'column_privileges' not in user:\n                        user['column_privileges'] = {}\n                    if db not in user['column_privileges']:\n                        user['column_privileges'][db] = {}\n                    if table not in user['column_privileges'][db]:\n                        user['column_privileges'][db][table] = {}\n                    if column not in user['column_privileges'][db][table]:\n                        user['column_privileges'][db][table][column] = []\n                    \n                    user['column_privileges'][db][table][column].extend(privileges)\n                    user['column_privileges'][db][table][column] = list(\n                        set(user['column_privileges'][db][table][column])\n                    )\n                    break\n            else:\n                return f\"ERROR: User '{username}' not found\"\n            \n            self.db._save_table_data('system', 'users', users)\n            \n            # Invalidate cache\n            self.permission_cache.invalidate_user(username)\n            \n            return f\"OK: Column privileges granted to '{username}'\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            return f\"ERROR: Failed to grant column privileges: {e}\"\n\n\ndef migrate_to_rbac(db: Database) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Migrate existing users to new RBAC system.\n    \n    Creates default roles and assigns users appropriately.\n    \"\"\"\n    auth = Authenticator(db)\n    \n    # Create default roles\n    auth.create_role(\"admin\", \"Full database access\", is_default=False)\n    auth.create_role(\"read_only\", \"Read-only access\", is_default=False)\n    auth.create_role(\"read_write\", \"Read and write access\", is_default=True)\n    \n    # Grant privileges to roles\n    auth.grant_privilege_to_role(\"admin\", \"*\", \"*\", [\"ALL\"])\n    auth.grant_privilege_to_role(\"read_only\", \"*\", \"*\", [\"SELECT\"])\n    auth.grant_privilege_to_role(\"read_write\", \"*\", \"*\", [\"SELECT\", \"INSERT\", \"UPDATE\", \"DELETE\"])\n    \n    # Migrate existing users\n    try:\n        users = db._get_table_data('system', 'users')\n        for user in users:\n            if 'roles' not in user:\n                user['roles'] = []\n            if 'column_privileges' not in user:\n                user['column_privileges'] = {}\n            \n            # Assign role based on is_admin\n            if user.get('is_admin'):\n                if 'admin' not in user['roles']:\n                    user['roles'].append('admin')\n        \n        db._save_table_data('system', 'users', users)\n        \n        return \"OK: Migration to RBAC completed successfully\"\n    except Exception as e:\n        return f\"ERROR: Migration failed: {e}\"\n</contentOfFile>\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 14, "timestamp": 1783713698.4049237, "date": "2026-07-10", "thinking": "", "prompt_tokens": 46446, "response_tokens": 5169} -->

<!-- {"role": "assistant", "content": "<WriteFile>\n<fileName>commands.py</fileName>\n<contentOfFile>\"\"\"\nCommand Execution Framework for LevelDB Socket Server\n\"\"\"\n\nimport os\nimport json\nimport time\nimport threading\nimport logging\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, Optional, List\n\n# Import audit logger for AUDIT LOG command\ntry:\n    from audit_logger import AuditLogger\n    AUDIT_AVAILABLE = True\nexcept ImportError:\n    AUDIT_AVAILABLE = False\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass CommandRegistry:\n    \"\"\"Registry for database commands with granular permission checking.\"\"\"\n    \n    # Command to privilege mapping\n    COMMAND_PRIVILEGES = {\n        'CREATE_DB': ['CREATE'],\n        'DROP_DB': ['DROP'],\n        'USE': [],  # No privilege required\n        'CREATE': ['CREATE'],\n        'DROP': ['DROP'],\n        'ALTER': ['ALTER'],\n        'INSERT': ['INSERT'],\n        'SELECT': ['SELECT'],\n        'UPDATE': ['UPDATE'],\n        'DELETE': ['DELETE'],\n        'BACKUP': ['ADMIN'],\n        'RESTORE': ['ADMIN'],\n        'CREATE_USER': ['ADMIN'],\n        'DROP_USER': ['ADMIN'],\n        'GRANT': ['ADMIN'],\n        'REVOKE': ['ADMIN'],\n        'CREATE_ROLE': ['ADMIN'],\n        'DROP_ROLE': ['ADMIN'],\n        'GRANT_ROLE': ['ADMIN'],\n        'REVOKE_ROLE': ['ADMIN'],\n        'SHOW': ['SELECT'],  # Can view with SELECT\n        'DESCRIBE': ['SELECT'],\n        'AUDIT_LOG': ['ADMIN'],\n    }\n    \n    def __init__(self, db, authenticator=None, replication_client=None):\n        self.db = db\n        self.authenticator = authenticator\n        self.replication_client = replication_client\n        self.commands = {\n            'CREATE_DB': self._create_db,\n            'DROP_DB': self._drop_db,\n            'USE': self._use_db,\n            'CREATE': self._create_table,\n            'DROP': self._drop_table,\n            'ALTER': self._alter_table,\n            'INSERT': self._insert,\n            'SELECT': self._select,\n            'UPDATE': self._update,\n            'DELETE': self._delete,\n            'SHOW': self._show,\n            'DESCRIBE': self._describe,\n            'BACKUP': self._backup,\n            'RESTORE': self._restore,\n            'CREATE_USER': self._create_user,\n            'DROP_USER': self._drop_user,\n            'GRANT': self._grant,\n            'REVOKE': self._revoke,\n            'CREATE_ROLE': self._create_role,\n            'DROP_ROLE': self._drop_role,\n            'GRANT_ROLE': self._grant_role,\n            'REVOKE_ROLE': self._revoke_role,\n            'SHOW_GRANTS': self._show_grants,\n            'SHOW_ROLES': self._show_roles,\n            'AUDIT_LOG': self._audit_log,\n        }\n    \n    def execute(self, cmd_type: str, params: Dict[str, Any], \n                client_state: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Execute a command with permission checking.\"\"\"\n        if cmd_type not in self.commands:\n            return f\"ERROR: Unknown command: {cmd_type}\"\n        \n        # Check permissions if authenticator available\n        if self.authenticator and 'session_token' in client_state:\n            token = client_state['session_token']\n            if not self._check_permission(cmd_type, params, client_state, token):\n                return \"ERROR: Permission denied\"\n        \n        return self.commands[cmd_type](params, client_state)\n    \n    def _check_permission(self, cmd_type: str, params: Dict[str, Any],\n                         client_state: Dict[str, Any], token: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if user has required permission for command.\"\"\"\n        # Get required privileges\n        required_privs = self.COMMAND_PRIVILEGES.get(cmd_type, [])\n        \n        # No privilege required\n        if not required_privs:\n            return True\n        \n        # Get database and table context\n        db_name = client_state.get('current_db', '')\n        table_name = params.get('table', '*')\n        \n        # Check if user has any of the required privileges\n        for priv in required_privs:\n            if self.authenticator.check_privilege(token, db_name, table_name, priv):\n                return True\n        \n        # Check column-level for UPDATE/SELECT if column specified\n        if cmd_type in ('UPDATE', 'SELECT') and 'column' in params:\n            column = params.get('column')\n            for priv in required_privs:\n                if self.authenticator.check_privilege(token, db_name, table_name, priv, column):\n                    return True\n        \n        return False\n    \n    def _create_db(self, params, state):\n        db_name = params.get('db_name')\n        if not db_name:\n            return \"ERROR: Database name required\"\n        return self.db.create_database(db_name)\n    \n    def _drop_db(self, params, state):\n        db_name = params.get('db_name')\n        if not db_name:\n            return \"ERROR: Database name required\"\n        return self.db.drop_database(db_name)\n    \n    def _use_db(self, params, state):\n        db_name = params.get('db_name')\n        if not db_name:\n            return \"ERROR: Database name required\"\n        \n        result = self.db.use_database(db_name)\n        if result.startswith(\"OK\"):\n            state['current_db'] = db_name\n        return result\n    \n    def _create_table(self, params, state):\n        if not state['current_db']:\n            return \"ERROR: No database selected. Use USE <database> first.\"\n        \n        table_name = params.get('table')\n        columns = params.get('columns', [])\n        \n        if not table_name:\n            return \"ERROR: Table name required\"\n        if not columns:\n            return \"ERROR: Column definitions required\"\n        \n        return self.db.create_table(state['current_db'], table_name, columns)\n    \n    def _drop_table(self, params, state):\n        if not state['current_db']:\n            return \"ERROR: No database selected\"\n        \n        table_name = params.get('table')\n        if not table_name:\n            return \"ERROR: Table name required\"\n        \n        return self.db.drop_table(state['current_db'], table_name)\n    \n    def _alter_table(self, params, state):\n        if not state['current_db']:\n            return \"ERROR: No database selected\"\n        \n        table_name = params.get('table')\n        alter_spec = params.get('alter_spec')\n        \n        if not table_name or not alter_spec:\n            return \"ERROR: ALTER TABLE requires table and specification\"\n        \n        return self.db.alter_table(state['current_db'], table_name, alter_spec)\n    \n    def _insert(self, params, state):\n        if not state['current_db']:\n            return \"ERROR: No database selected\"\n        \n        table_name = params.get('table')\n        values = params.get('values', [])\n        \n        if not table_name:\n            return \"ERROR: Table name required\"\n        if not values:\n            return \"ERROR: Values required\"\n        \n        result = self.db.insert(state['current_db'], table_name, values)\n        \n        # Replicate if configured\n        if self.replication_client and result.startswith(\"OK\"):\n            try:\n                self.replication_client.replicate_command(\n                    f\"INSERT INTO {table_name} VALUES {values}\"\n                )\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Replication failed: {e}\")\n        \n        return result\n    \n    def _select(self, params, state):\n        if not state['current_db']:\n            return \"ERROR: No database selected\"\n        \n        table_name = params.get('table')\n        columns = params.get('columns', ['*'])\n        where = params.get('where')\n        \n        if not table_name:\n            return \"ERROR: Table name required\"\n        \n        return self.db.select(state['current_db'], table_name, columns, where)\n    \n    def _update(self, params, state):\n        if not state['current_db']:\n            return \"ERROR: No database selected\"\n        \n        table_name = params.get('table')\n        set_clause = params.get('set')\n        where = params.get('where')\n        \n        if not table_name or not set_clause:\n            return \"ERROR: UPDATE requires table and SET clause\"\n        \n        result = self.db.update(state['current_db'], table_name, set_clause, where)\n        \n        if self.replication_client and result.startswith(\"OK\"):\n            try:\n                where_clause = f\" WHERE {where}\" if where else \"\"\n                self.replication_client.replicate_command(\n                    f\"UPDATE {table_name} SET {set_clause}{where_clause}\"\n                )\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Replication failed: {e}\")\n        \n        return result\n    \n    def _delete(self, params, state):\n        if not state['current_db']:\n            return \"ERROR: No database selected\"\n        \n        table_name = params.get('table')\n        where = params.get('where')\n        \n        if not table_name:\n            return \"ERROR: Table name required\"\n        \n        result = self.db.delete(state['current_db'], table_name, where)\n        \n        if self.replication_client and result.startswith(\"OK\"):\n            try:\n                where_clause = f\" WHERE {where}\" if where else \"\"\n                self.replication_client.replicate_command(\n                    f\"DELETE FROM {table_name}{where_clause}\"\n                )\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Replication failed: {e}\")\n        \n        return result\n    \n    def _show(self, params, state):\n        target = params.get('target', 'DATABASES')\n        \n        if target.upper() == 'DATABASES':\n            return self.db.show_databases()\n        elif target.upper() == 'TABLES':\n            if not state['current_db']:\n                return \"ERROR: No database selected\"\n            return self.db.show_tables(state['current_db'])\n        elif target.upper() == 'USERS':\n            if not state.get('is_admin'):\n                return \"ERROR: Only admins can list users\"\n            return self.db.show_users()\n        else:\n            return f\"ERROR: Unknown SHOW target: {target}\"\n    \n    def _describe(self, params, state):\n        if not state['current_db']:\n            return \"ERROR: No database selected\"\n        \n        table_name = params.get('table')\n        if not table_name:\n            return \"ERROR: Table name required\"\n        \n        return self.db.describe_table(state['current_db'], table_name)\n    \n    def _backup(self, params, state):\n        db_name = params.get('db_name') or state.get('current_db')\n        backup_path = params.get('backup_path')\n        \n        if not db_name:\n            return \"ERROR: No database specified\"\n        \n        return self.db.backup_database(db_name, backup_path)\n    \n    def _restore(self, params, state):\n        db_name = params.get('db_name')\n        backup_path = params.get('backup_path')\n        \n        if not db_name or not backup_path:\n            return \"ERROR: RESTORE requires database name and backup path\"\n        \n        return self.db.restore_database(db_name, backup_path)\n    \n    def _create_user(self, params, state):\n        username = params.get('username')\n        password = params.get('password')\n        \n        if not username or not password:\n            return \"ERROR: CREATE USER requires username and password\"\n        \n        return self.db.create_user(username, password)\n    \n    def _drop_user(self, params, state):\n        username = params.get('username')\n        if not username:\n            return \"ERROR: DROP USER requires username\"\n        \n        return self.db.drop_user(username)\n    \n    def _grant(self, params, state):\n        username = params.get('username')\n        db_name = params.get('db')\n        table = params.get('table')\n        privileges = params.get('privileges', [])\n        \n        if not all([username, db_name, table, privileges]):\n            return \"ERROR: GRANT syntax: GRANT privilege ON db.table TO username\"\n        \n        return self.db.grant_privilege(username, db_name, table, privileges)\n    \n    def _revoke(self, params, state):\n        username = params.get('username')\n        db_name = params.get('db')\n        table = params.get('table')\n        privileges = params.get('privileges', [])\n        \n        if not all([username, db_name, table, privileges]):\n            return \"ERROR: REVOKE syntax: REVOKE privilege ON db.table FROM username\"\n        \n        return self.db.revoke_privilege(username, db_name, table, privileges)\n    \n    def _create_role(self, params, state):\n        if not self.authenticator:\n            return \"ERROR: Role management not available\"\n        \n        role_name = params.get('role_name')\n        description = params.get('description', '')\n        \n        if not role_name:\n            return \"ERROR: CREATE ROLE requires role name\"\n        \n        return self.authenticator.create_role(role_name, description)\n    \n    def _drop_role(self, params, state):\n        if not self.authenticator:\n            return \"ERROR: Role management not available\"\n        \n        role_name = params.get('role_name')\n        if not role_name:\n            return \"ERROR: DROP ROLE requires role name\"\n        \n        return self.authenticator.drop_role(role_name)\n    \n    def _grant_role(self, params, state):\n        if not self.authenticator:\n            return \"ERROR: Role management not available\"\n        \n        username = params.get('username')\n        role_name = params.get('role_name')\n        \n        if not username or not role_name:\n            return \"ERROR: GRANT ROLE syntax: GRANT ROLE rolename TO username\"\n        \n        return self.authenticator.grant_role_to_user(username, role_name)\n    \n    def _revoke_role(self, params, state):\n        if not self.authenticator:\n            return \"ERROR: Role management not available\"\n        \n        username = params.get('username')\n        role_name = params.get('role_name')\n        \n        if not username or not role_name:\n            return \"ERROR: REVOKE ROLE syntax: REVOKE ROLE rolename FROM username\"\n        \n        return self.authenticator.revoke_role_from_user(username, role_name)\n    \n    def _show_grants(self, params, state):\n        if not self.authenticator:\n            return \"ERROR: Grant information not available\"\n        \n        username = params.get('username')\n        return self.authenticator.show_grants(username)\n    \n    def _show_roles(self, params, state):\n        if not self.authenticator:\n            return \"ERROR: Role information not available\"\n        \n        return self.authenticator.show_roles()\n    \n    def _audit_log(self, params, state):\n        \"\"\"Query audit log entries (admin only).\"\"\"\n        if not state.get('is_admin'):\n            return \"ERROR: AUDIT LOG requires admin privileges\"\n        \n        if not AUDIT_AVAILABLE:\n            return \"ERROR: Audit logging not available\"\n        \n        limit = params.get('limit', 50)\n        user = params.get('user')\n        action = params.get('action')\n        \n        try:\n            return f\"OK: Audit log query - limit={limit}, user={user}, action={action}\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            return f\"ERROR: Failed to query audit log: {e}\"\n</contentOfFile>\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 14, "timestamp": <!-- {"role": "assistant", "content": "<WriteFile>\n<fileName>parser.py</fileName>\n<contentOfFile>\"\"\"\nSQL-like Command Parser for LevelDB Socket Server\n\nSupports parsing of various SQL-like commands for database operations.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport re\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, Tuple, Optional, List\n\n\nclass CommandParser:\n    \"\"\"Parser for SQL-like commands.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self):\n        # Initialize command patterns\n        self.patterns = {\n            'CREATE_DB': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*CREATE\\s+DATABASE\\s+(?P<db_name>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'DROP_DB': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*DROP\\s+DATABASE\\s+(?P<db_name>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'USE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*USE\\s+(?P<db_name>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'CREATE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*CREATE\\s+TABLE\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)\\s*\\((?P<columns>[^)]+)\\)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'DROP': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*DROP\\s+TABLE\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'ALTER': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*ALTER\\s+TABLE\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)\\s+(?P<alter_spec>.+)$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'INSERT': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*INSERT\\s+INTO\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)\\s+VALUES\\s*\\((?P<values>[^)]+)\\)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'SELECT': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*SELECT\\s+(?P<columns>[\\w\\s,]+)\\s+FROM\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)(?:\\s+WHERE\\s+(?P<where>.+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'UPDATE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*UPDATE\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)\\s+SET\\s+(?P<set>[\\w\\s=,]+)(?:\\s+WHERE\\s+(?P<where>.+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'DELETE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*DELETE\\s+FROM\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)(?:\\s+WHERE\\s+(?P<where>.+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'SHOW': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*SHOW\\s+(?P<target>DATABASES|TABLES|USERS)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'DESCRIBE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*DESCRIBE\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'CREATE_USER': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*CREATE\\s+USER\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s+PASSWORD\\s+(?P<password>\\S+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'DROP_USER': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*DROP\\s+USER\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'GRANT': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*GRANT\\s+(?P<privileges>[\\w\\s,]+)\\s+ON\\s+(?P<db>\\w+)\\.(?P<table>\\w+)\\s+TO\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'REVOKE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*REVOKE\\s+(?P<privileges>[\\w\\s,]+)\\s+ON\\s+(?P<db>\\w+)\\.(?P<table>\\w+)\\s+FROM\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'CREATE_ROLE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*CREATE\\s+ROLE\\s+(?P<role_name>\\w+)(?:\\s+DESCRIPTION\\s+(?P<description>\\'[^\\']*\\'|\\\"[^\\\"]*\\\"))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'DROP_ROLE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*DROP\\s+ROLE\\s+(?P<role_name>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'GRANT_ROLE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*GRANT\\s+ROLE\\s+(?P<role_name>\\w+)\\s+TO\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'REVOKE_ROLE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*REVOKE\\s+ROLE\\s+(?P<role_name>\\w+)\\s+FROM\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'SHOW_GRANTS': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*SHOW\\s+GRANTS(?:\\s+FOR\\s+(?P<username>\\w+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'SHOW_ROLES': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*SHOW\\s+ROLES\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'AUDIT_LOG': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*AUDIT\\s+LOG(?:\\s+LIMIT\\s+(?P<limit>\\d+))?(?:\\s+USER\\s+(?P<user>\\w+))?(?:\\s+ACTION\\s+(?P<action>\\w+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n        }\n    \n    def parse(self, sql: str) -> Tuple[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse SQL-like command and return command type and parameters.\n        \n        Args:\n            sql: SQL command string\n        \n        Returns:\n            Tuple of (command_type, parameters_dict)\n        \"\"\"\n        sql = sql.strip()\n        \n        # Try each pattern\n        for cmd_type, pattern in self.patterns.items():\n            match = pattern.match(sql)\n            if match:\n                params = match.groupdict()\n                \n                # Parse special fields\n                if 'columns' in params and params['columns']:\n                    params['columns'] = [c.strip() for c in params['columns'].split(',')]\n                \n                if 'values' in params and params['values']:\n                    params['values'] = self._parse_values(params['values'])\n                \n                if 'privileges' in params and params['privileges']:\n                    params['privileges'] = [p.strip().upper() for p in params['privileges'].split(',')]\n                \n                if 'description' in params and params['description']:\n                    # Remove quotes\n                    params['description'] = params['description'].strip('\\'\"')\n                \n                if 'limit' in params and params['limit']:\n                    params['limit'] = int(params['limit'])\n                \n                return cmd_type, params\n        \n        # Check for simple commands\n        sql_upper = sql.upper()\n        if sql_upper == 'QUIT' or sql_upper == 'EXIT':\n            return 'QUIT', {}\n        \n        return 'UNKNOWN', {'sql': sql}\n    \n    def _parse_values(self, values_str: str) -> List[Any]:\n        \"\"\"Parse VALUES clause.\"\"\"\n        values = []\n        for val in values_str.split(','):\n            val = val.strip()\n            # Try to convert to number\n            try:\n                if '.' in val:\n                    values.append(float(val))\n                else:\n                    values.append(int(val))\n            except ValueError:\n                # Remove quotes if present\n                if (val.startswith(\"'\") and val.endswith(\"'\")) or \\\n                   (val.startswith('\"') and val.endswith('\"')):\n                    val = val[1:-1]\n                values.append(val)\n        return values\n\n\nclass BackupRestoreParser:\n    \"\"\"Parser for backup and restore commands.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self):\n        self.patterns = {\n            'BACKUP': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*BACKUP\\s+DATABASE\\s+(?P<db_name>\\w+)(?:\\s+TO\\s+(?P<backup_path>\\S+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'RESTORE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*RESTORE\\s+DATABASE\\s+(?P<db_name>\\w+)\\s+FROM\\s+(?P<backup_path>\\S+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n        }\n    \n    def parse(self, sql: str) -> Tuple[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n        \"\"\"Parse backup/restore commands.\"\"\"\n        sql = sql.strip()\n        \n        for cmd_type, pattern in self.patterns.items():\n            match = pattern.match(sql)\n            if match:\n                return cmd_type, match.groupdict()\n        \n        # Fall back to regular parser\n        regular_parser = CommandParser()\n        return regular_parser.parse(sql)\n\n\n# Example usage\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    parser = CommandParser()\n    \n    test_commands = [\n        \"CREATE DATABASE testdb\",\n        \"USE testdb\",\n        \"CREATE TABLE users (id INT, name TEXT)\",\n        \"INSERT INTO users VALUES (1, 'Alice')\",\n        \"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = 1\",\n        \"UPDATE users SET name = 'Bob' WHERE id = 1\",\n        \"DELETE FROM users WHERE id = 1\",\n        \"DROP TABLE users\",\n        \"DROP DATABASE testdb\",\n        \"GRANT SELECT, INSERT ON testdb.users TO alice\",\n        \"REVOKE DELETE ON testdb.users FROM alice\",\n        \"CREATE ROLE readonly DESCRIPTION 'Read-only access'\",\n        \"GRANT ROLE readonly TO alice\",\n        \"SHOW GRANTS FOR alice\",\n        \"SHOW ROLES\",\n    ]\n    \n    for cmd in test_commands:\n        cmd_type, params = parser.parse(cmd)\n        print(f\"Command: {cmd}\")\n        print(f\"  Type: {cmd_type}\")\n        print(f\"  Params: {params}\")\n        print()\n</contentOfFile>\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 14, "timestamp": 1783713758.1449523, "date": "2026-07-10", rotation.\"\"\"\n        self.key_rotations += 1\n    \n    def get_overhead_ratio(self) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Calculate encryption overhead ratio.\"\"\"\n        if self.total_bytes_encrypted == 0:\n            return 0.0\n        # AES-GCM adds 16 bytes tag + 12 bytes nonce\n        overhead = self.total_encryptions * 28\n        return overhead / (self.total_bytes_encrypted + overhead)\n    \n    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Convert to dictionary.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            'total_encryptions': self.total_encryptions,\n            'total_decryptions': self.total_decryptions,\n            'total_bytes_encrypted': self.total_bytes_encrypted,\n            'total_bytes_decrypted': self.total_bytes_decrypted,\n            'encryption_time_ms': round(self.encryption_time_ms, 3),\n            'decryption_time_ms': round(self.decryption_time_ms, 3),\n            'key_rotations': self.key_rotations,\n            'overhead_ratio': round(self.get_overhead_ratio() * 100, 2)\n        }\n\n\nclass KeyManager:\n    \"\"\"Manages encryption keys with key derivation and rotation.\"\"\"\n    \n    # AES-256 requires 32-byte key\n    KEY_SIZE = 32\n    # PBKDF2 iterations\n    PBKDF2_ITERATIONS = 100000\n    # Salt size for key derivation\n    SALT_SIZE = 32\n    \n    def __init__(self, key_file: Optional[str] = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize key manager.\n        \n        Args:\n            key_file: Path to store/load master key\n        \"\"\"\n        if not CRYPTO_AVAILABLE:\n            raise ImportError(\"cryptography library required. Install with: pip install cryptography\")\n        \n        self.key_file = key_file\n        self._master_key: Optional[bytes] = None\n        self._salt: Optional[bytes] = None\n        self._key_id: str = \"\"\n    \n    def generate_key(self) -> bytes:\n        \"\"\"Generate a random 256-bit key.\"\"\"\n        return secrets.token_bytes(self.KEY_SIZE)\n    \n    def derive_key(self, passphrase: str, salt: Optional[bytes] = None) -> Tuple[bytes, bytes]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Derive key from passphrase using PBKDF2.\n        \n        Args:\n            passphrase: User passphrase\n            salt: Optional salt (generated if None)\n        \n        Returns:\n            Tuple of (derived_key, salt)\n        \"\"\"\n        if salt is None:\n            salt = secrets.token_bytes(self.SALT_SIZE)\n        \n        kdf = PBKDF2(\n            algorithm=hashes.SHA256(),\n            length=self.KEY_SIZE,\n            salt=salt,\n            iterations=self.PBKDF2_ITERATIONS,\n            backend=default_backend()\n        )\n        \n        key = kdf.derive(passphrase.encode('utf-8'))\n        return key, salt\n    \n    def load_or_create_key(self, passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> bytes:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load existing key or create new one.\n        \n        Args:\n            passphrase: Optional passphrase for key derivation\n        \n        Returns:\n            Master key bytes\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.key_file and os.path.exists(self.key_file):\n            return self._load_key_file(passphrase)\n        \n        # Generate new key\n        if passphrase:\n            key, salt = self.derive_key(passphrase)\n            self._salt = salt\n        else:\n            key = self.generate_key()\n            self._salt = None\n        \n        self._master_key = key\n        self._key_id = hashlib.sha256(key).hexdigest()[:16]\n        \n        if self.key_file:\n            self._save_key_file(passphrase)\n        \n        return key\n    \n    def _load_key_file(self, passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> bytes:\n        \"\"\"Load key from file.\"\"\"\n        with open(self.key_file, 'rb') as f:\n            data = f.read()\n        \n        # Check if it's a derived key (has salt) or raw key\n        if len(data) > self.KEY_SIZE:\n            # Has salt - derived key\n            salt = data[:self.SALT_SIZE]\n            stored_key_hash = data[self.SALT_SIZE:]\n            \n            if not passphrase:\n                raise ValueError(\"Passphrase required to unlock key file\")\n            \n            key, _ = self.derive_key(passphrase, salt)\n            key_hash = hashlib.sha256(key).digest()\n            \n            if key_hash != stored_key_hash:\n                raise ValueError(\"Invalid passphrase\")\n            \n            self._salt = salt\n            self._master_key = key\n        else:\n            # Raw key\n            self._master_key = data\n            self._salt = None\n        \n        self._key_id = hashlib.sha256(self._master_key).hexdigest()[:16]\n        return self._master_key\n    \n    def _save_key_file(self, passphrase: Optional[str] = None):\n        \"\"\"Save key to file.\"\"\"\n        os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(self.key_file), exist_ok=True)\n        \n        if self._salt:\n            # Store salt + hash of derived key\n            key_hash = hashlib.sha256(self._master_key).digest()\n            data = self._salt + key_hash\n        else:\n            # Store raw key\n            data = self._master_key\n        \n        with open(self.key_file, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(data)\n        \n        # Restrict permissions\n        os.chmod(self.key_file, 0o600)\n    \n    def rotate_key(self, new_passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> Tuple[bytes, bytes]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate new key for key rotation.\n        \n        Returns:\n            Tuple of (old_key, new_key)\n        \"\"\"\n        old_key = self._master_key\n        \n        if new_passphrase:\n            new_key, new_salt = self.derive_key(new_passphrase)\n            self._salt = new_salt\n        else:\n            new_key = self.generate_key()\n        \n        self._master_key = new_key\n        self._key_id = hashlib.sha256(new_key).hexdigest()[:16]\n        \n        if self.key_file:\n            self._save_key_file(new_passphrase if new_passphrase else None)\n        \n        return old_key, new_key\n    \n    @property\n    def master_key(self) -> Optional[bytes]:\n        \"\"\"Get current master key.\"\"\"\n        return self._master_key\n    \n    @property\n    def key_id(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get current key ID.\"\"\"\n        return self._key_id\n\n\nclass EncryptedDatabase:\n    \"\"\"\n    Transparent encryption wrapper around Database class.\n    \n    Encrypts all data using AES-256-GCM before storing in LevelDB.\n    \"\"\"\n    \n    # Version marker for encrypted data\n    ENCRYPTION_VERSION = 1\n    # Header size: version(1) + key_id_len(1) + key_id + nonce(12) + tag(16)\n    HEADER_OVERHEAD = 30\n    \n    def __init__(self, data_dir: str, server_id: int = 1,\n                 encryption_key: Optional[Union[str, bytes]] = None,\n                 key_file: Optional[str] = None,\n                 passphrase: Optional[str] = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize encrypted database.\n        \n        Args:\n            data_dir: Directory for database files\n            server_id: Unique server ID\n            encryption_key: Raw encryption key (32 bytes) or hex string\n            key_file: Path to key file\n            passphrase: Passphrase for key derivation\n        \"\"\"\n        if not CRYPTO_AVAILABLE:\n            raise ImportError(\"cryptography library required. Install with: pip install cryptography\")\n        \n        self.data_dir = data_dir\n        self.server_id = server_id\n        self.metrics = EncryptionMetrics()\n        \n        # Initialize key manager\n        self.key_manager = KeyManager(key_file)\n        \n        if encryption_key:\n            # Use provided key\n            if isinstance(encryption_key, str):\n                encryption_key = bytes.fromhex(encryption_key)\n            self.key_manager._master_key = encryption_key\n            self.key_manager._key_id = hashlib.sha256(encryption_key).hexdigest()[:16]\n        elif passphrase:\n            # Derive key from passphrase\n            self.key_manager.load_or_create_key(passphrase)\n        elif key_file:\n            # Load from file\n            self.key_manager.load_or_create_key()\n        else:\n            raise ValueError(\"Must provide encryption_key, key_file, or passphrase\")\n        \n        # Initialize underlying database\n        # Use subdirectory for encrypted data to coexist with regular databases\n        encrypted_dir = os.path.join(data_dir, 'encrypted')\n        self.db = Database(encrypted_dir, server_id)\n        \n        # Initialize AES-GCM\n        self._init_cipher()\n        \n        logger.info(f\"[EncryptedDB] Initialized with key_id={self.key_manager.key_id}\")\n    \n    def _init_cipher(self):\n        \"\"\"Initialize AES-GCM cipher.\"\"\"\n        self.cipher = AESGCM(self.key_manager.master_key)\n    \n    def _encrypt(self, plaintext: bytes) -> bytes:\n        \"\"\"\n        Encrypt data with AES-256-GCM.\n        \n        Format: version(1) | key_id_len(1) | key_id | nonce(12) | ciphertext | tag(16)\n        \"\"\"\n        start_time = time.time()\n        \n        # Generate random nonce\n        nonce = secrets.token_bytes(12)\n        \n        # Encrypt\n        ciphertext = self.cipher.encrypt(nonce, plaintext, None)\n        \n        # Build header\n        key_id_bytes = self.key_manager.key_id.encode('utf-8')\n        header = struct.pack(\n            '!BB',\n            self.ENCRYPTION_VERSION,\n            len(key_id_bytes)\n        ) + key_id_bytes + nonce\n        \n        # Combine\n        result = header + ciphertext\n        \n        # Record metrics\n        duration_ms = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000\n        self.metrics.record_encryption(len(plaintext), duration_ms)\n        \n        return result\n    \n    def _decrypt(self, ciphertext: bytes) -> bytes:\n        \"\"\"\n        Decrypt data with AES-256-GCM.\n        \"\"\"\n        start_time = time.time()\n        \n        # Parse header\n        version = ciphertext[0]\n        if version != self.ENCRYPTION_VERSION:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Unsupported encryption version: {version}\")\n        \n        key_id_len = ciphertext[1]\n        header_len = 2 + key_id_len + 12  # version + len + key_id + nonce\n        \n        key_id = ciphertext[2:2+key_id_len].decode('utf-8')\n        nonce = ciphertext[2+key_id_len:header_len]\n        encrypted_data = ciphertext[header_len:]\n        \n        # Check key ID (for key rotation support)\n        if key_id != self.key_manager.key_id:\n            # TODO: Try decrypting with old keys\n            logger.warning(f\"[EncryptedDB] Data encrypted with different key: {key_id}\")\n        \n        # Decrypt\n        plaintext = self.cipher.decrypt(nonce, encrypted_data, None)\n        \n        # Record metrics\n        duration_ms = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000\n        self.metrics.record_decryption(len(plaintext), duration_ms)\n        \n        return plaintext\n    \n    def _encode_value(self, value: Any) -> bytes:\n        \"\"\"Encode value to bytes.\"\"\"\n        if isinstance(value, bytes):\n            return value\n        return json.dumps(value).encode('utf-8')\n    \n    def _decode_value(self, data: bytes) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Decode bytes to value.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return json.loads(data.decode('utf-8'))\n        except:\n            return data\n    \n    def put(self, key: str, value: Any) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Store encrypted value.\n        \n        Args:\n            key: Plaintext key\n            value: Value to encrypt and store\n        \n        Returns:\n            True if successful\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            value_bytes = self._encode_value(value)\n            encrypted = self._encrypt(value_bytes)\n            self.db.put(key, encrypted)\n            return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"[EncryptedDB] Encryption failed: {e}\")\n            return False\n    \n    def get(self, key: str) -> Optional[Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Retrieve and decrypt value.\n        \n        Args:\n            key: Plaintext key\n        \n        Returns:\n            Decrypted value or None\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            encrypted = self.db.get(key)\n            if encrypted is None:\n                return None\n            \n            # Handle non-encrypted data (migration support)\n            if isinstance(encrypted, bytes) and len(encrypted) > 0:\n                if encrypted[0] != self.ENCRYPTION_VERSION:\n                    # Assume unencrypted\n                    return self._decode_value(encrypted)\n            \n            decrypted = self._decrypt(encrypted)\n            return self._decode_value(decrypted)\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"[EncryptedDB] Decryption failed: {e}\")\n            return None\n    \n    def delete(self, key: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Delete key.\"\"\"\n        return self.db.delete(key)\n    \n    def rotate_key(self, new_key: Optional[bytes] = None,\n                   new_passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Rotate encryption key and re-encrypt all data.\n        \n        Args:\n            new_key: New encryption key (optional)\n            new_passphrase: New passphrase (optional)\n        \n        Returns:\n            True if successful\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            logger.info(\"[EncryptedDB] Starting key rotation...\")\n            start_time = time.time()\n            \n            # Generate new key\n            if new_key:\n                old_key, new_key = self.key_manager._master_key, new_key\n                self.key_manager._master_key = new_key\n                self.key_manager._key_id = hashlib.sha256(new_key).hexdigest()[:16]\n            else:\n                old_key, new_key = self.key_manager.rotate_key(new_passphrase)\n            \n            # Re-encrypt all data\n            old_cipher = AESGCM(old_key)\n            new_cipher = AESGCM(new_key)\n            \n            # Scan all keys\n            all_keys = self.db.keys()\n            rotated_count = 0\n            \n            for key in all_keys:\n                try:\n                    encrypted = self.db.get(key)\n                    if encrypted is None:\n                        continue\n                    \n                    # Parse header\n                    version = encrypted[0]\n                    if version != self.ENCRYPTION_VERSION:\n                        continue  # Skip non-encrypted\n                    \n                    key_id_len = encrypted[1]\n                    header_len = 2 + key_id_len + 12\n                    \n                    old_nonce = encrypted[2+key_id_len:header_len]\n                    old_ciphertext = encrypted[header_len:]\n                    \n                    # Decrypt with old key\n                    plaintext = old_cipher.decrypt(old_nonce, old_ciphertext, None)\n                    \n                    # Re-encrypt with new key\n                    new_nonce = secrets.token_bytes(12)\n                    new_ciphertext = new_cipher.encrypt(new_nonce, plaintext, None)\n                    \n                    # Build new header\n                    new_key_id = self.key_manager.key_id.encode('utf-8')\n                    new_header = struct.pack(\nn                        '!BB',\\n                        self.ENCRYPTION_VERSION,\\n                        len(new_key_id)\\n                    ) + new_key_id + new_nonce\\n                    \\n                    new_encrypted = new_header + new_ciphertext\\n                    self.db.put(key, new_encrypted)\\n                    rotated_count += 1\\n                    \\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    logger.error(f\\\"[EncryptedDB] Failed to rotate key for {key}: {e}\\\")\\n            \\n            # Update cipher\\n            self.cipher = new_cipher\\n            \\n            duration = time.time() - start_time\\n            self.metrics.record_key_rotation()\\n            \\n            logger.info(f\\\"[EncryptedDB] Key rotation complete: {rotated_count} items in {duration:.2f}s\\\")\\n            return True\\n            \\n        except Exception as e:\\n            logger.error(f\\\"[EncryptedDB] Key rotation failed: {e}\\\")\\n            return False\\n    \\n    def get_metrics(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Get encryption metrics.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        return self.metrics.to_dict()\\n    \\n    def close(self):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Close database.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        self.db.close()\\n    \\n    # Delegate other methods to underlying database\\n    def __getattr__(self, name):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Delegate unknown methods to underlying database.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        return getattr(self.db, name)\\n\\n\\ndef create_encrypted_database(\\n    data_dir: str,\\n    encryption_key: Optional[str] = None,\\n    passphrase: Optional[str] = None,\\n    key_file: Optional[str] = None,\\n    server_id: int = 1\\n) -> EncryptedDatabase:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Factory function to create encrypted database.\\n    \\n    Args:\\n        data_dir: Database directory\\n        encryption_key: Hex-encoded encryption key\\n        passphrase: Passphrase for key derivation\\n        key_file: Path to key file\\n        server_id: Server ID\\n    \\n    Returns:\\n        EncryptedDatabase instance\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    return EncryptedDatabase(\\n        data_dir=data_dir,\\n        server_id=server_id,\\n        encryption_key=encryption_key,\\n        key_file=key_file,\\n        passphrase=passphrase\\n    )\\n</contentOfFile>\\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 15, "timestamp": 1783713821.667541, "date": "2026-07-10", "thinking": "", "prompt_tokens": 59985, "response_tokens": 4227} -->

cipher.\"\"\"\n        self.cipher = AESGCM(self.key_manager.master_key)\n    \n    def _encrypt(self, plaintext: bytes) -> bytes:\n        \"\"\"\n        Encrypt data with AES-256-GCM.\n        \n encrypted = self.db.get(key)\n            if encrypted is None:\n                return None\n            \n            # Handle non-encrypted data (migration support)\n            if logger.error(f\"[EncryptedDB] Decryption failed: {e}\")\n            return None\n    \n    def delete(self, key: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Delete key.\"\"\"\n        return self.db.delete(key)\n data.\n        \n        Args:\n            new_key: New encryption key (optional)\n            new_passphrase: New passphrase (optional)\n        \n        Returns:\n            True if successful\n  \n                    # Parse header\n                    version = encrypted[0]\n                    if version != self.ENCRYPTION_VERSION:\n                        continue  # Skip non-encrypted\n                    \n                    key_id_len = encrypted[1]\n                    header_len = 2 + key_id_len + 12\n                    \n                    old_nonce = encrypted[2+key_id_len:header_len]\n                    old_ciphertext = encrypted[header_len:]\n                    \n                    # Decrypt with old key\n                    plaintext = old_cipher.decrypt(old_nonce, old_ciphertext, None)\n                    \n                    # Re-encrypt with new key\n                    new_nonce = secrets.token_bytes(12)\n                    new_ciphertext = new_cipher.encrypt(new_nonce, plaintext, None)\n                    \n                    # Build new header\n                    new_key_id = self.key_manager.key_id.encode('utf-8')\n                    new_header = struct.pack(\nn                        '!BB',\\n                        self.ENCRYPTION_VERSION,\\n                        len(new_key_id)\\n                    ) + new_key_id + new_nonce\\n                    \\n                    new_encrypted = new_header + new_ciphertext\\n                    self.db.put(key, new_encrypted)\\n                    rotated_count += 1\\n                    \\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    logger.error(f\\\"[EncryptedDB] Failed to rotate key for {key}: {e}\\\")\\n            \\n            # Update cipher\\n            self.cipher = new_cipher\\n            \\n            duration = time.time() - start_time\\n            self.metrics.record_key_rotation()\\n            \\n            logger.info(f\\\"[EncryptedDB] Key rotation complete: {rotated_count} items in {duration:.2f}s\\\")\\n            return True\\n            \\n        except Exception as e:\\n            logger.error(f\\\"[EncryptedDB] Key rotation failed: {e}\\\")\\n            return False\\n    \\n    def get_metrics(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Get encryption metrics.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        return self.metrics.to_dict()\\n    \\n    def close(self):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Close database.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        self.db.close()\\n
