  Store encrypted value.\n        \n        Args:\n            key: Plaintext key\n            value: Value to encrypt and store\n        \n        Returns:\n            True if successful\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            value_bytes = self._encode_value(value)\n            encrypted = self._encrypt(value_bytes)\n            self.db.put(key, encrypted)\n            return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"[EncryptedDB] Encryption failed: {e}\")\n            return False\n    \n    def get(self, key: str) -> Optional[Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Retrieve and decrypt value.\n        \n        Args:\n            key: Plaintext key\n        \n        Returns:\n            Decrypted value or None\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            encrypted = self.db.get(key)\n            if encrypted is None:\n                return None\n            \n            # Handle non-encrypted data (migration support)\n            if isinstance(encrypted, bytes) and len(encrypted) > 0:\n                if encrypted[0] != self.ENCRYPTION_VERSION:\n                    # Assume unencrypted\n                    return self._decode_value(encrypted)\n            \n            decrypted = self._decrypt(encrypted)\n            return self._decode_value(decrypted)\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"[EncryptedDB] Decryption failed: {e}\")\n            return None\n    \n    def delete(self, key: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Delete key.\"\"\"\n        return self.db.delete(key)\n    \n    def rotate_key(self, new_key: Optional[bytes] = None,\n                   new_passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Rotate encryption key and re-encrypt all data.\n        \n        Args:\n            new_key: New encryption key (optional)\n            new_passphrase: New passphrase (optional)\n        \n        Returns:\n            True if successful\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            logger.info(\"[EncryptedDB] Starting key rotation...\")\n            start_time = time.time()\n            \n            # Generate new key\n            if new_key:\n                old_key, new_key = self.key_manager._master_key, new_key\n                self.key_manager._master_key = new_key\n                self.key_manager._key_id = hashlib.sha256(new_key).hexdigest()[:16]\n            else:\n                old_key, new_key = self.key_manager.rotate_key(new_passphrase)\n            \n            # Re-encrypt all data\n            old_cipher = AESGCM(old_key)\n            new_cipher = AESGCM(new_key)\n            \n            # Scan all keys\n            all_keys = self.db.keys()\n            rotated_count = 0\n            \n            for key in all_keys:\n                try:\n                    encrypted = self.db.get(key)\n                    if encrypted is None:\n                        continue\n                    \n                    # Parse header\n                    version = encrypted[0]\n                    if version != self.ENCRYPTION_VERSION:\n                        continue  # Skip non-encrypted\n                    \n                    key_id_len = encrypted[1]\n                    header_len = 2 + key_id_len + 12\n                    \n                    old_nonce = encrypted[2+key_id_len:header_len]\n                    old_ciphertext = encrypted[header_len:]\n                    \n                    # Decrypt with old key\n                    plaintext = old_cipher.decrypt(old_nonce, old_ciphertext, None)\n                    \n                    # Re-encrypt with new key\n                    new_nonce = secrets.token_bytes(12)\n                    new_ciphertext = new_cipher.encrypt(new_nonce, plaintext, None)\n                    \n                    # Build new header\n                    new_key_id = self.key_manager.key_id.encode('utf-8')\n                    new_header = struct.pack(\n                        '!BB',\n                        self.ENCRYPTION_VERSION,\n                        len(new_key_id)\n                    ) + new_key_id + new_nonce\n                    \n                    new_encrypted = new_header + new_ciphertext\n                    self.db.put(key, new_encrypted)\n                    rotated_count += 1\n                    \n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.error(f\"[EncryptedDB] Failed to rotate key for {key}: {e}\")\n            \n            # Update cipher\n            self.cipher = new_cipher\n            \n            duration = time.time() - start_time\n            self.metrics.record_key_rotation()\n            \n            logger.info(f\"[EncryptedDB] Key rotation complete: {rotated_count} items in {duration:.2f}s\")\n            return True\n            \n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"[EncryptedDB] Key rotation failed: {e}\")\n            return False\n    \n    def get_metrics(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Get encryption metrics.\"\"\"\n        return self.metrics.to_dict()\n    \n    def close(self):\n        \"\"\"Close database.\"\"\"\n        self.db.close()\n    \n    # Delegate other methods to underlying database\n    def __getattr__(self, name):\n        \"\"\"Delegate unknown methods to underlying database.\"\"\"\n        return getattr(self.db, name)\n\n\ndef create_encrypted_database(\n    data_dir: str,\n    encryption_key: Optional[str] = None,\n    passphrase: Optional[str] = None,\n    key_file: Optional[str] = None,\n    server_id: int = 1\n) -> EncryptedDatabase:\n    \"\"\"\n    Factory function to create encrypted database.\n    \n    Args:\n        data_dir: Database directory\n        encryption_key: Hex-encoded encryption key\n        passphrase: Passphrase for key derivation\n        key_file: Path to key file\n        server_id: Server ID\n    \n    Returns:\n        EncryptedDatabase instance\n    \"\"\"\n    return EncryptedDatabase(\n        data_dir=data_dir,\n        server_id=server_id,\n        encryption_key=encryption_key,\n        key_file=key_file,\n        passphrase=passphrase\n    )\n</contentOfFile>\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 15, "timestamp": 1783713889.6197448, "date": "2026-07-10", "thinking": "", "prompt_tokens": 68141, "response_tokens": 3967} -->

    km._master_key = km.generate_key()\n        km._key_id = \"test\"\n        \n        old_key, new_key = km.rotate_key()\n        self.assertNotEqual(old_key, new_key)\n metrics.record_encryption(1000, 1.0)\n        \n        # 28 bytes overhead per encryption\n        ratio = metrics.get_overhead_ratio()\n        self.assertGreater(ratio, 0)\n        self.assertLess(ratio, 0.1)  # Should be small for 1000 bytes\n    \n    def test_to_dict(self):\n        \"\"\"Test metrics export.\"\"\"\n        metrics = EncryptionMetrics()\n        metrics.record_encryption(100, 1.0)\n        metrics.record_decryption(100, 2.0)\n        \n        data = metrics.to_dict()\n        self.assertEqual(data['total_encryptions'], 1)\n        self.assertEqual(data['total_decryptions'], 1)\n\n\n@unittest.skipUnless(CRYPTO_AVAILABLE, \"cryptography library not available\")\nclass TestEncryptedDatabase(unittest.TestCase):\n    \"\"\"Test EncryptedDatabase class.\"\"\"\n    \n    def setUp(self):\n        self.temp_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp()\n    \n    def tearDown(self):\n        import shutil\n        shutil.rmtree(self.temp_dir, ignore_errors=True)\n    \n    def test_encrypt_decrypt(self):\n        \"\"\"Test basic encryption and decryption.\"\"\"\n        db = EncryptedDatabase(\n            self.temp_dir,\n retrieving string.\"\"\"\n        db = EncryptedDatabase(\n            self.temp_dir,\n            passphrase=\"testpassphrase\"\n        )\n        \n        db.put(\"key1\", \"value1\")\n        result = db.get(\"key1\")\n        \n        self.assertEqual(result, \"value1\")\n        db.close()\n    \n    def test_put_get_dict(self):\n        \"\"\"Test storing and retrieving dictionary.\"\"\"\n        db = EncryptedDatabase(\n            self.temp_dir,\n            passphrase=\"testpassphrase\"\n        )\n        \n        data = {\"name\": \"Alice\", \"age\": 30}\n        db.put(\"user1\", data)\n        result = db.get(\"user1\")\n        \n        self.assertEqual(result, data)\n        db.close()\n    \n    def test_delete(self):\n        \"\"\"Test delete operation.\"\"\"\n        db = EncryptedDatabase(\n            self.temp_dir,\n            passphrase=\"testpassphrase\"\n        )\n        \n        db.put(\"key1\", \"value1\")\n collection.\"\"\"\n        db = EncryptedDatabase(\n            self.temp_dir,\n            passphrase=\"testpassphrase\"\n        )\n        \n        db.put(\"key1\", \"value1\")\n db.close()\n    \n    def test_with_hex_key(self):\n        \"\"\"Test with hex-encoded key.\"\"\"\n        import secrets\n        key = secrets.token_hex(32)\n        \n        db = ignore_errors=True)\n    \n    def test_key_rotation(self):\n        \"\"\"Test rotating encryption key.\"\"\"\n        db = EncryptedDatabase(\n            self.temp_dir,\n            passphrase=\"oldpassphrase\"\n        )\n        \n        # Store some data\n        db.put(\"key1\", \"value1\")\n        db.put(\"key2\", \"value2\")\n        \n        # Rotate key\n        result shutil.rmtree(self.temp_dir, ignore_errors=True)\n    \n    def test_coexistence(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that encrypted and regular DB can coexist.\"\"\"\n        from database import Database\n <!-- {"role": "tool", "content": "\"\"\"\nBackup utilities with integrity checking for LevelDB Socket Server\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport gzip\nimport hashlib\nimport os\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, Optional, Tuple\n\n\nclass BackupIntegrityError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Raised when backup integrity check fails.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\ndef calculate_checksum(data: bytes) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Calculate SHA-256 checksum of data.\"\"\"\n    return hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()\n\n\ndef verify_backup_integrity(file_path: str) -> Tuple[bool, Optional[str]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify backup file integrity.\n    Returns (success, error_message)\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Check file exists and is readable\n        if not os.path.exists(file_path):\n            return False, f\"File not found: {file_path}\"\n        \n        if not os.access(file_path, os.R_OK):\n            return False, f\"File not readable: {file_path}\"\n        \n        # Try to decompress and parse\n        with gzip.open(file_path, 'rt', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n            content = f.read()\n            backup_data = json.loads(content)\n        \n        # Verify required fields\n        if 'version' not in backup_data:\n            return False, \"Missing 'version' field\"\n        \n        if 'tables' not in backup_data and 'table' not in backup_data:\n            return False, \"Missing 'tables' or 'table' field\"\n        \n        # Verify checksum if present\n        if 'checksum' in backup_data:\n            stored_checksum = backup_data['checksum']\n            # Recalculate checksum without the checksum field\n            verification_data = {k: v for k, v in backup_data.items() if k != 'checksum'}\n            expected = calculate_checksum(json.dumps(verification_data, sort_keys=True).encode())\n            if stored_checksum != expected:\n                return False, \"Checksum mismatch - file may be corrupted\"\n        \n        # Verify table data integrity\n        if 'tables' in backup_data:\n            for table_name, table_info in backup_data['tables'].items():\n                if 'schema' not in table_info:\n                    return False, f\"Table '{table_name}' missing schema\"\n                if 'rows' not in table_info:\n                    return False, f\"Table '{table_name}' missing rows\"\n        elif 'table' in backup_data:\n            # Single table backup\n            if 'schema' not in backup_data:\n                return False, \"Single table backup missing schema\"\n            if 'rows' not in backup_data:\n                return False, \"Single table backup missing rows\"\n        \n        return True, None\n        \n    except gzip.BadGzipFile:\n        return False, \"Invalid gzip file - may be corrupted\"\n    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n        return False, f\"Invalid JSON: {e}\"\n    except Exception as e:\n        return False, f\"Integrity check failed: {e}\"\n\n\ndef create_backup_metadata(db_name: str, tables: list, rows_count: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Create metadata for backup file.\"\"\"\n    return {\n        'version': '1.0',\n        'created_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),\n        'database': db_name,\n        'table_count': len(tables),\n        'row_count': rows_count,\n        'tables': tables\n    }\n\n\ndef add_integrity_check(backup_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Add checksum to backup data for integrity verification.\"\"\"\n    # Create a copy without checksum for calculation\n    data_for_hash = {k: v for k, v in backup_data.items() if k != 'checksum'}\n    data_bytes = json.dumps(data_for_hash, sort_keys=True).encode()\n    backup_data['checksum'] = calculate_checksum(data_bytes)\n    return backup_data\n\n\ndef validate_before_restore(file_path: str, target_db: str) -> Tuple[bool, Optional[str]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate backup before restore.\n    Returns (success, error_message)\n    \"\"\"\n    valid, error = verify_backup_integrity(file_path)\n    if not valid:\n        return False, error\n    \n    try:\n        with gzip.open(file_path, 'rt', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n            backup_data = json.load(f)\n        \n        # Check if target database matches\n        source_db = backup_data.get('database')\n        if source_db and source_db != target_db:\n            # This is a warning, not an error\n            pass  # Allow restoring to different database name\n        \n        return True, None\n        \n    except Exception as e:\n        return False, f\"Validation failed: {e}\"\n\n\ndef get_backup_info(file_path: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"Get information about a backup file.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        stat = os.stat(file_path)\n        \n        with gzip.open(file_path, 'rt', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n            backup_data = json.load(f)\n        \n        info = {\n            'file': os.path.basename(file_path),\n            'path': file_path,\n            'size': stat.st_size,\n            'size_human': format_size(stat.st_size),\n            'modified': datetime.fromtimestamp(stat.st_mtime).isoformat(),\n            'version': backup_data.get('version', 'unknown'),\n            'database': backup_data.get('database', 'unknown'),\n            'table_count': backup_data.get('table_count', len(backup_data.get('tables', {}))),\n            'row_count': backup_data.get('row_count', 0),\n            'has_checksum': 'checksum' in backup_data\n        }\n        \n        # Calculate row count if not stored\n        if info['row_count'] == 0 and 'tables' in backup_data:\n            for table_data in backup_data['tables'].values():\n                info['row_count'] += len(table_data.get('rows', []))\n        \n        return info\n        \n    except Exception:\n        return None\n\n\ndef format_size(size_bytes: int) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Format byte size to human readable.\"\"\"\n    if size_bytes < 1024:\n        return f\"{size_bytes} B\"\n    elif size_bytes < 1024 * 1024:\n        return f\"{size_bytes / 1024:.1f} KB\"\n    elif size_bytes < 1024 * 1024 * 1024:\n        return f\"{size_bytes / (1024 * 1024):.1f} MB\"\n    else:\n        return f\"{size_bytes / (1024 * 1024 * 1024):.1f} GB\"\n\n\nclass BackupManager:\n    \"\"\"Manage backup operations with integrity checking.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, backup_dir: str = './backups'):\n        self.backup_dir = backup_dir\n        os.makedirs(backup_dir, exist_ok=True)\n    \n    def list_backups(self) -> list:\n        \"\"\"List all backup files with info.\"\"\"\n        backups = []\n        for filename in os.listdir(self.backup_dir):\n            if filename.endswith('.json.gz'):\n                file_path = os.path.join(self.backup_dir, filename)\n                info = get_backup_info(file_path)\n                if info:\n                    backups.append(info)\n        return sorted(backups, key=lambda x: x['modified'], reverse=True)\n    \n    def cleanup_old_backups(self, keep_count: int = 10):\n        \"\"\"Remove old backups keeping only the most recent.\"\"\"\n        backups = self.list_backups()\n        if len(backups) > keep_count:\n            for backup in backups[keep_count:]:\n                try:\n                    os.remove(backup['path'])\n                except:\n                    pass\n\n\ndef generate_backup_filename(db_name: str, suffix: str = '') -> str:\n    \"\"\"Generate standardized backup filename.\"\"\"\n    timestamp = datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')\n    if suffix:\n        return f\"{db_name}_{suffix}_{timestamp}.json.gz\"\n    return f\"{db_name}_{timestamp}.json.gz\"\n", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 16, "timestamp": 1783713980.8005157, "date": "2026-07-10", "name": "ReadFile"} Server\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport gzip\nimport hashlib\nimport os\nimport struct\nimport secrets\nimport logging\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, Optional, Tuple, Union, BinaryIO\nfrom enum import Enum\n\n# Try to import optional compression libraries\ntry:\n    import lz4.frame\n    LZ4_AVAILABLE = True\nexcept ImportError:\n    LZ4_AVAILABLE = False\n\ntry:\n    import zstandard as zstd\n    ZSTD_AVAILABLE = True\nexcept ImportError:\n    ZSTD_AVAILABLE = False\n\n# Try to import cryptography\ntry:\n    from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.aead import AESGCM\n    from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.kdf.pbkdf2 import PBKDF2\n    from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import hashes\n    from cryptography.hazmat.backends import default_backend\n    CRYPTO_AVAILABLE = True\nexcept ImportError:\n    CRYPTO_AVAILABLE = False\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass CompressionType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Supported compression algorithms.\"\"\"\n    GZIP = \"gzip\"\n    LZ4 = \"lz4\"\n    ZSTD = \"zstd\"\n    NONE = \"none\"\n\n\nclass BackupIntegrityError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Raised when backup integrity check fails.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass BackupEncryptionError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Raised when encryption/decryption fails.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\ndef calculate_checksum(data: bytes) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Calculate SHA-256 checksum of data.\"\"\"\n    return hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()\n\n\ndef derive_key(passphrase: str, salt: Optional[bytes] = None) -> Tuple[bytes, bytes]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Derive encryption key from passphrase using PBKDF2.\n    \n    Args:\n        passphrase: User passphrase\n        salt: Optional salt (generated if None)\n    \n    Returns:\n        Tuple of (derived_key, salt)\n    \"\"\"\n    if not CRYPTO_AVAILABLE:\n        raise ImportError(\"cryptography library required for encryption\")\n    \n    if salt is None:\n        salt = secrets.token_bytes(32)\n    \n    kdf = PBKDF2(\n        algorithm=hashes.SHA256(),\n        length=32,  # 256-bit key\n        salt=salt,\n        iterations=100000,\n        backend=default_backend()\n    )\n    \n    key = kdf.derive(passphrase.encode('utf-8'))\n    return key, salt\n\n\ndef encrypt_data(plaintext: bytes, passphrase: str) -> bytes:\n    \"\"\"\n    Encrypt data with AES-256-GCM.\n    \n    Format: salt(32) + nonce(12) + ciphertext + tag(16)\n    \"\"\"\n    if not CRYPTO_AVAILABLE:\n        raise BackupEncryptionError(\"cryptography library not available\")\n    \n    key, salt = derive_key(passphrase)\n    nonce = secrets.token_bytes(12)\n    cipher = AESGCM(key)\n    \n    ciphertext = cipher.encrypt(nonce, plaintext, None)\n    \n    # Combine: salt + nonce + ciphertext\n    return salt + nonce + ciphertext\n\n\ndef decrypt_data(ciphertext: bytes, passphrase: str) -> bytes:\n    \"\"\"\n    Decrypt data encrypted with AES-256-GCM.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not CRYPTO_AVAILABLE:\n        raise BackupEncryptionError(\"cryptography library not available\")\n    \n    if len(ciphertext) < 60:  # salt(32) + nonce(12) + minimum tag(16)\n        raise BackupEncryptionError(\"Invalid encrypted data\")\n    \n    salt = ciphertext[:32]\\n    encrypted_data = ciphertext[32:]\n    \\n    key, _ = derive_key(passphrase, salt)\n    cipher = AESGCM(key)\n    \\n    try:\n        return cipher.decrypt(encrypted_data[:12], encrypted_data[12:], None)\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise BackupEncryptionError(f\"Decryption failed: {e}\")\n\n\ndef compress_data(data: bytes, compression: CompressionType = CompressionType.GZIP,\n                  level: int = 6) -> bytes:\n    \"\"\"\n    Compress data using specified algorithm.\n    \n    Args:\n        data: Data to compress\n        compression: Compression algorithm\n        level: Compression level (1-9 for gzip, 1-12 for others)\n    \\n    Returns:\nn        Compressed data\\n    \"\"\"\n    if compression == CompressionType.NONE:\n        return data\n    \n    if compression == CompressionType.GZIP:\n        import io\n        buf = io.BytesIO()\n        with gzip.GzipFile(fileobj=buf, mode='wb', compresslevel=level) as f:\n            f.write(data)\n        return buf.getvalue()\n    \n    elif compression == CompressionType.LZ4:\n        if not LZ4_AVAILABLE:\n            logger.warning(\"LZ4 not available, falling back to gzip\")\n            return compress_data(data, CompressionType.GZIP, level)\n        return lz4.frame.compress(data, compression_level=level)\n    \n    elif compression == CompressionType.ZSTD:\n        if not ZSTD_AVAILABLE:\n            logger.warning(\"Zstd not available, falling back to gzip\")\n            return compress_data(data, CompressionType.GZIP, level)\n        cctx = zstd.ZstdCompressor(level=level)\n        return cctx.compress(data)\n    \n    else:\n        return data\n\n\ndef decompress_data(data: bytes, compression: CompressionType = CompressionType.GZIP) -> bytes:\n    \"\"\"\n    Decompress data using specified algorithm.\n    \"\"\"\n    if compression == CompressionType.NONE:\n        return data\n    \n    if compression == CompressionType.GZIP:\n        import io\n        buf = io.BytesIO(data)\n        with gzip.GzipFile(fileobj=buf, mode='rb') as f:\n            return f.read()\n    \n    elif compression == CompressionType.LZ4:\n        if not LZ4_AVAILABLE:\n            raise BackupIntegrityError(\"LZ4 not available for decompression\")\n        return lz4.frame.decompress(data)\n    \n    elif compression == CompressionType.ZSTD:\n        if not ZSTD_AVAILABLE:\n            raise BackupIntegrityError(\"Zstd not available for decompression\")\n        dctx = zstd.ZstdDecompressor()\n        return dctx.decompress(data)\n    \n    else:\n        return data\n\n\ndef detect_compression(data: bytes) -> CompressionType:\n    \"\"\"Detect compression type from data magic bytes.\"\"\"\n    if len(data) < 4:\\n        return CompressionType.NONE\\n    \\n    # Gzip magic: 1f 8b\\n    if data[:2] == b'\\\\x1f\\\\x8b':\\n        return CompressionType.GZIP\\n    \\n    # LZ4 magic: 04 22 4d 18\\n    if data[:4] == b'\\\\x04\\\\x22\\\\x4d\\\\x18':\\n        return CompressionType.LZ4\\n    \\n    # Zstd magic: 28 b5 2f fd\\n    if data[:4] == b'\\\\x28\\\\xb5\\\\x2f\\\\xfd':\\n        return CompressionType.ZSTD\\n    \\n    return CompressionType.NONE\n\n\ndef verify_backup_integrity(file_path: str, passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> Tuple[bool, Optional[str]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify backup file integrity.\n    \n    Args:\n        file_path: Path to backup file\n        passphrase: Optional passphrase for encrypted backups\n    \n    Returns:\n        Tuple of (success, error_message)\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Check file exists and is readable\n        if not os.path.exists(file_path):\n            return False, f\"File not found: {file_path}\"\n        \n        if not os.access(file_path, os.R_OK):\n            return False, f\"File not readable: {file_path}\"\n        \n        # Read raw file\n        with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:\\n            raw_data = f.read()\\n        \\n        # Check for encryption marker (first byte 0xFF)\\n        is_encrypted = len(raw_data) > 0 and raw_data[0] == 0xFF\\n        \\n        if is_encrypted:\\n            if not passphrase:\\n                return False, \"Backup is encrypted but no passphrase provided\"\\n            if not CRYPTO_AVAILABLE:\\n                return False, \"Encryption library not available\"\\n            \\n            # Remove encryption marker and decrypt\\n            encrypted_data = raw_data[1:]\\n            try:\\n                decrypted = decrypt_data(encrypted_data, passphrase)\\n                raw_data = decrypted\\n            except BackupEncryptionError as e:\\n                return False, f\"Decryption failed: {e}\\\"\\n        \\n        # Detect and decompress\\n        compression = detect_compression(raw_data)\\n        try:\\n            decompressed = decompress_data(raw_data, compression)\\n        except Exception as e:\\n            return False, f\"Decompression failed: {e}\\\"\\n        \\n        # Parse JSON\\n        try:\\n            backup_data = json.loads(decompressed.decode('utf-8'))\\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\\n            return False, f\"Invalid JSON: {e}\\\"\\n        \\n        # Verify required fields\\n        if 'version' not in backup_data:\\n            return False, \"Missing 'version' field\\\"\\n        \\n        if 'tables' not in backup_data and 'table' not in backup_data:\\n            return False, \"Missing 'tables' or 'table' field\\\"\\n        \\n        # Verify checksum if present\\n        if 'checksum' in backup_data:\\n            stored_checksum = backup_data['checksum']\\n            verification_data = {k: v for k, v in backup_data.items() if k != 'checksum'}\\n            expected = calculate_checksum(json.dumps(verification_data, sort_keys=True).encode())\\n            if stored_checksum != expected:\\n                return False, \\\"Checksum mismatch - file may be corrupted\\\"\\n        \\n        # Verify table data integrity\\n        if 'tables' in backup_data:\\n            for table_name, table_info in backup_data['tables'].items():\\n                if 'schema' not in table_info:\\n                    return False, f\\\"Table '{table_name}' missing schema\\\"\\n                if 'rows' not in table_info:\\n                    return False, f\\\"Table '{table_name}' missing rows\\\"\\n        elif 'table' in backup_data:\\n            if 'schema' not in backup_data:\\n                return False, \\\"Single table backup missing schema\\\"\\n            if 'rows' not in backup_data:\\n                return False, \\\"Single table backup missing rows\\\"\\n        \\n        return True, None\\n        \\n    except Exception as e:\\n        return False, f\\\"Integrity check failed: {e}\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef create_backup(db_name: str, tables_data: Dict[str, Any],\\n                  compression: CompressionType = CompressionType.GZIP,\\n                  compression_level: int = 6,\\n                  passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> bytes:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Create backup with optional compression and encryption.\\n    \\n    Args:\\n        db_name: Database name\\n        tables_data: Table data to backup\\n        compression: Compression algorithm\\n        compression_level: Compression level\\n        passphrase: Optional passphrase for encryption\\n    \\n    Returns:\\n        Backup data as bytes\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    # Create backup structure\\n    backup_data = {\\n        'version': '2.0',\\n        'created_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),\\n        'database': db_name,\\n        'table_count': len(tables_data),\\n        'compression': compression.value,\\n        'encrypted': passphrase is not None,\\n        'tables': tables_data\\n    }\\n    \\n    # Calculate row count\\n    row_count = 0\\n    for table_info in tables_data.values():\\n        row_count += len(table_info.get('rows', []))\\n    backup_data['row_count'] = row_count\\n    \\n    # Add integrity checksum\\n    data_for_hash = {k: v for k, v in backup_data.items() if k != 'checksum'}\\n    backup_data['checksum'] = calculate_checksum(json.dumps(data_for_hash, sort_keys=True).encode())\\n    \\n    # Serialize to JSON\\n    json_data = json.dumps(backup_data, sort_keys=True).encode('utf-8')\\n    \\n    # Compress\\n    compressed = compress_data(json_data, compression, compression_level)\\n    \\n    # Encrypt if passphrase provided\\n    if passphrase:\\n        if not CRYPTO_AVAILABLE:\\n            raise BackupEncryptionError(\\\"cryptography library not available\\\")\\n        encrypted = encrypt_data(compressed, passphrase)\\n        # Add encryption marker\\n        return b'\\\\xff' + encrypted\\n    \\n    return compressed\\n\\n\\ndef restore_backup(file_path: str, passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Restore backup from file.\\n    \\n    Args:\\n        file_path: Path to backup file\\n        passphrase: Optional passphrase for encrypted backups\\n    \\n    Returns:\\n        Restored backup data\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    # Read file\\n    with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:\\n        raw_data = f.read()\\n    \\n    # Check for encryption\\n    is_encrypted = len(raw_data) > 0 and raw_data[0] == 0xFF\\n    \\n    if is_encrypted:\\n        if not passphrase:\\n            raise BackupEncryptionError(\\\"Backup is encrypted but no passphrase provided\\\")\\n        if not CRYPTO_AVAILABLE:\\n            raise BackupEncryptionError(\\\"Encryption library not available\\\")\\n        \\n        encrypted_data = raw_data[1:]\\n        raw_data = decrypt_data(encrypted_data, passphrase)\\n    \\n    # Detect and decompress\\n    compression = detect_compression(raw_data)\\n    decompressed = decompress_data(raw_data, compression)\\n    \\n    # Parse JSON\\n    backup_data = json.loads(decompressed.decode('utf-8'))\\n    \\n    # Verify checksum\\n    if 'checksum' in backup_data:\\n        stored_checksum = backup_data['checksum']\\n        verification_data = {k: v for k, v in backup_data.items() if k != 'checksum'}\\n        expected = calculate_checksum(json.dumps(verification_data, sort_keys=True).encode())\\n        if stored_checksum != expected:\\n            raise BackupIntegrityError(\\\"Checksum mismatch - backup may be corrupted\\\")\\n    \\n    return backup_data\\n\\n\\ndef create_backup_metadata(db_name: str, tables: list, rows_count: int,\\n                           compression: CompressionType = '2.0',\\n        'created_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),\\n        'database': db_name,\\n        'table_count': len(tables),\\n        'row_count': rows_count,\\n        'tables': tables,\\n        'compression': compression.value,\\n        'encrypted': encrypted\\n    }\\n\\n\\ndef add_integrity_check(backup_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Add checksum to backup data for integrity verification.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    data_for_hash = {k: v for k, v in backup_data.items() if k != 'checksum'}\\n    data_bytes = json.dumps(data_for_hash, sort_keys=True).encode()\\n    backup_data['checksum'] = calculate_checksum(data_bytes)\\n    return backup_data\\n\\n\\ndef validate_before_restore(file_path: str, target_db: str,\\n                           passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> Tuple[bool, Optional[str]]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Validate backup before restore.\\n    \\n    Returns:\\n        Tuple of (success, error_message)\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    valid, error = verify_backup_integrity(file_path, passphrase)\\n    if not valid:\\n        return False, error\\n    \\n    try:\\n        backup_data = restore_backup(file_path, passphrase)\\n logger.warning(f\\\"Restoring from '{source_db}' to '{target_db}'\\\")\\n        \\n        return True, None\\n        \\n    except Exception as e:\\n        return False, f\\\"Validation failed: {e}\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef get_backup_info(file_path: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Get information about a backup file.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    try:\\n        stat = os.stat(file_path)\\n        \\n        # Read first few bytes to detect encryption and compression\\n        with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:\\n            header = f.read(100)\\n        \\n        is_encrypted = len(header) > 0 and header[0] == 0xFF\\n        compression = detect_compression(header[1:] if is_encrypted else header)\\n        \\n        info = {\\n            'file': os.path.basename(file_path),\\n            'path': file_path,\\n            'size': stat.st_size,\\n            'size_human': format_size(stat.st_size),\\n            'modified': datetime.fromtimestamp(stat.st_mtime).isoformat(),\\n            'encrypted': is_encrypted,\\n            'compression': compression.value\\n        }\\n        \\n        # Try to read metadata\\n        try:\\n            backup_data = restore_backup(file_path) if not is_encrypted else None\\n            if backup_data:\\n                info.update({\\n                    'version': backup_data.get('version', 'unknown'),\\n                    'database': backup_data.get('database', 'unknown'),\\n                    'table_count': backup_data.get('table_count', 0),\\n                    'row_count': backup_data.get('row_count', 0),\\n                    'has_checksum': 'checksum' in backup_data\\n                })\\n        except:\\n            pass\\n        \\n        return info\\n        \\n    except Exception:\\n        return None\\n\\n\\ndef format_size(size_bytes: int) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Format byte size to human readable.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    if size_bytes < 1024:\\n        return f\\\"{size_bytes} B\\\"\\n    elif size_bytes < 1024 * 1024:\\n        return f\\\"{size_bytes / 1024:.1f} KB\\\"\\n    elif size_bytes < 1024 * 1024 * 1024:\\n        return f\\\"{size_bytes / (1024 * 1024):.1f} MB\\\"\\n    else:\\n        return f\\\"{size_bytes / (1024 * 1024 * 1024):.1f} GB\\\"\\n\\n\\nclass BackupManager:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Manage backup operations with integrity checking.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    \\n    def __init__(self, backup_dir: str = './backups'):\\n        self.backup_dir = backup_dir\\n        os.makedirs(backup_dir, exist_ok=True)\\n    \\n    def create_backup(self, db_name: str, tables_data: Dict[str, Any],\\n                     compression: str = 'gzip',\\n                     compression_level: int = 6,\\n                     passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> str:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Create backup and save to file.\\n        \\n        Returns:\\n            Path to backup file\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        comp_type = CompressionType(compression) if compression else CompressionType.GZIP\\n        \\n        backup_data = create_backup(\\n            db_name, tables_data,\\n            compression=comp_type,\\n            compression_level=compression_level,\\n            passphrase=passphrase\\n        )\\n        \\n        filename = generate_backup_filename(db_name, encrypted=passphrase is not None)\\n        file_path = os.path.join(self.backup_dir, filename)\\n        \\n        with open(file_path, 'wb') as f:\\n            f.write(backup_data)\\n        \\n        return file_path\\n    \\n    def restore_backup(self, filename: str, passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Restore backup from file.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        file_path = os.path.join(self.backup_dir, filename)\\n        return restore_backup(file_path, passphrase)\\n    \\n    def list_backups(self) -> list:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"List all backup files with info.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        backups = []\\n        for filename in os.listdir(self.backup_dir):\\n            if filename.endswith('.json.gz') or filename.endswith('.enc'):\\n                file_path = os.path.join(self.backup_dir, filename)\\n                info = get_backup_info(file_path)\\n                if info:\\n                    backups.append(info)\\n        return sorted(backups, key=lambda x: x['modified'], reverse=True)\\n    \\n    def cleanup_old_backups(self, keep_count: int = 10):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Remove old backups keeping only the most recent.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        backups = self.list_backups()\\n        if len(backups) > keep_count:\\n            for backup in backups[keep_count:]:\\n                try:\\n                    os.remove(backup['path'])\\n                except:\\n                    pass\\n\\n\\ndef generate_backup_filename(db_name: str, suffix: str = '',\\n                            encrypted: bool = False) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Generate standardized backup filename.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    timestamp = datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')\\n    ext = '.enc' if encrypted else '.json.gz'\\n    if suffix:\\n        return f\\\"{db_name}_{suffix}_{timestamp}{ext}\\\"\\n    return f\\\"{db_name}_{timestamp}{ext}\\\"\\n\\n\\n# Backward compatibility\\n__all__ = [\\n    'BackupIntegrityError',\\n    'BackupEncryptionError',\\n    'CompressionType',\\n    'BackupManager',\\n    'calculate_checksum',\\n    'verify_backup_integrity',\\n    'create_backup',\\n    'restore_backup',\\n    'create_backup_metadata',\\n    'add_integrity_check',\\n    'validate_before_restore',\\n    'get_backup_info',\\n    'format_size',\\n    'generate_backup_filename',\\n    'compress_data',\\n    'decompress_data',\\n    'encrypt_data',\\n    'decrypt_data'\\n]\n</contentOfFile>\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 16, "timestamp": 1783714023.7865484, "date": "2026-07-10", "thinking": \"\"\"Calculate SHA-256 checksum of data.\"\"\"\n    return hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()\n\n\ndef derive_key(passphrase: str, salt: Optional[bytes] = None) -> Tuple[bytes, bytes]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Derive encryption key from passphrase using PBKDF2.\n    \n    Args:\n        passphrase: User passphrase\n        salt: Optional salt (generated if None)\n    \n    Returns:\n        Tuple of (derived_key, salt)\n    \"\"\"\n    if not CRYPTO_AVAILABLE:\n        raise ImportError(\"cryptography library required for encryption\")\n    \n    if salt is None:\n        salt = backend=default_backend()\n    )\n    \n    key = kdf.derive(passphrase.encode('utf-8'))\n    return key, salt\n\n\ndef encrypt_data(plaintext: bytes, passphrase: str) -> bytes:\n    \"\"\"\n available\")\n    \n    key, salt = derive_key(passphrase)\n    nonce = secrets.token_bytes(12)\n    cipher = AESGCM(key)\n    \n    ciphertext = cipher.encrypt(nonce, plaintext, None)\n    \n    # Combine: salt + nonce + ciphertext\n    return salt + nonce + ciphertext\n\n\ndef decrypt_data(ciphertext: bytes, passphrase: str) -> bytes:\n    \"\"\"\n    Decrypt data encrypted with AES-256-GCM.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not CRYPTO_AVAILABLE:\n        raise BackupEncryptionError(\"cryptography library not available\")\n    \n    if len(ciphertext) < 60:  # salt(32) + nonce(12) + minimum tag(16)\n AESGCM(key)\n    \n    try:\n        return cipher.decrypt(encrypted_data[:12], encrypted_data[12:], None)\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise BackupEncryptionError(f\"Decryption failed: {e}\")\n\n\ndef compress_data(data: bytes, compression: CompressionType = CompressionType.GZIP,\n                  level: int = 6) -> bytes:\n    \"\"\"\n    Compress data using specified algorithm.\n data\n    \"\"\"\n    if compression == CompressionType.NONE:\n        return data\n    \n    if compression == CompressionType.GZIP:\n        import io\n        buf = io.BytesIO()\n        with decompress_data(data: bytes, compression: CompressionType = CompressionType.GZIP) -> bytes:\n    \"\"\"\n    Decompress data using specified algorithm.\n    \"\"\"\n    if compression == \"\"\"Detect compression type from data magic bytes.\"\"\"\n    if len(data) < 4:\n        return CompressionType.NONE\n    \n    # Gzip magic: 1f 8b\n    if data[:2] == b'\\x1f\\x8b':\n        return b'\\x28\\xb5\\x2f\\xfd':\n        return CompressionType.ZSTD\n    \n    return CompressionType.NONE\n\n\ndef verify_backup_integrity(file_path: str, passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> Tuple[bool, Optional[str]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify backup file integrity.\n    \n    Args:\n        file_path: Path to backup file\n        passphrase: Optional passphrase for encrypted backups\n    \n found: {file_path}\"\n        \n        if not os.access(file_path, os.R_OK):\n            return False, f\"File not readable: {file_path}\"\n        \n        # Read raw file\n        with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:\n            raw_data = f.read()\n        \n        # Check for encryption marker (first byte 0xFF)\n        is_encrypted = len(raw_data) > 0 and raw_data[0] == 0xFF\n        \n        if is_encrypted:\n            if not passphrase:\n                return False, \"Backup is encrypted but no passphrase provided\"\n            if not CRYPTO_AVAILABLE:\n compression_level: int = 6,\n                  passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> bytes:\n    \"\"\"\n    Create backup with optional compression and encryption.\n    \n    Args:\n        db_name: encryption\n    \n    Returns:\n        Backup data as bytes\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create backup structure\n    backup_data = {\n        'version': '2.0',\n        'created_at': 'tables': tables_data\n    }\n    \n    # Calculate row count\n    row_count = 0\n    for table_info in tables_data.values():\n        row_count += len(table_info.get('rows', []))\n calculate_checksum(json.dumps(data_for_hash, sort_keys=True).encode())\n    \n    # Serialize to JSON\n    json_data = json.dumps(backup_data, sort_keys=True).encode('utf-8')\n    \n    # Compress\n    compressed = compress_data(json_data, compression, compression_level)\n    \n    # Encrypt if passphrase provided\n    if passphrase:\n        if not CRYPTO_AVAILABLE:\n            raise return compressed\n\n\ndef restore_backup(file_path: str, passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Restore backup from file.\n    \n    Args:\n        file_path: Path to backup file\n        passphrase: Optional passphrase for encrypted backups\n    \n    Returns:\n        Restored backup data\n    \"\"\"\n    # Read file\n    with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:\n stored_checksum = backup_data['checksum']\n        verification_data = {k: v for k, v in backup_data.items() if k != 'checksum'}\n        expected = calculate_checksum(json.dumps(verification_data, sort_keys=True).encode())\n        if stored_checksum != expected:\n            raise BackupIntegrityError(\"Checksum mismatch - backup may be corrupted\")\n    \n    return backup_data\n\n\ndef create_backup_metadata(db_name: str, tables: list, rows_count: int,\n                           compression: CompressionType = CompressionType.GZIP,\n                           encrypted: bool = False) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Create metadata for backup file.\"\"\"\n    return {\n        'version': '2.0',\n        'created_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),\n        'database': db_name,\n        'table_count': len(tables),\n        'row_count': rows_count,\n        'tables': tables,\n        'compression': compression.value,\n        'encrypted': encrypted\n    }\n\n\ndef add_integrity_check(backup_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Add checksum to backup data for integrity verification.\"\"\"\n    data_for_hash = {k: v for k, v in backup_data.items() if k != 'checksum'}\n    data_bytes = json.dumps(data_for_hash, sort_keys=True).encode()\n    backup_data['checksum'] = calculate_checksum(data_bytes)\n    return backup_data\n\n\ndef validate_before_restore(file_path: str, target_db: str,\n                           passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> Tuple[bool, Optional[str]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate backup before restore.\n    \n    Returns:\n        Tuple of (success, error_message)\n    \"\"\"\n    valid, error = verify_backup_integrity(file_path, passphrase)\n    if not valid:\n        return False, error\n    \n    try:\n        backup_data = restore_backup(file_path, passphrase)\n        \n        # Check if target database matches (warning only)\n        source_db = backup_data.get('database')\n        if source_db and source_db != target_db:\n            logger.warning(f\"Restoring from '{source_db}' to '{target_db}'\")\n        \n        return True, None\n        \n    except Exception as e:\n        return False, f\"Validation failed: {e}\"\n\n\ndef get_backup_info(file_path: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"Get information about a backup file.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        stat = os.stat(file_path)\n        \n        # Read first few bytes to detect encryption and compression\n        with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:\n            header = f.read(100)\n        \n        is_encrypted = len(header) > 0 and header[0] == 0xFF\n        compression = detect_compression(header[1:] if is_encrypted else header)\n        \n        info = {\n            'file': os.path.basename(file_path),\n            'path': file_path,\n            'size': stat.st_size,\n            'size_human': format_size(stat.st_size),\n            'modified': datetime.fromtimestamp(stat.st_mtime).isoformat(),\n            'encrypted': is_encrypted,\n            'compression': compression.value\n        }\n        \n        # Try to read metadata\n        try:\n            backup_data = restore_backup(file_path) if not is_encrypted else None\n            if backup_data:\n                info.update({\n                    'version': backup_data.get('version', 'unknown'),\n                    'database': backup_data.get('database', 'unknown'),\n                    'table_count': backup_data.get('table_count', 0),\n                    'row_count': backup_data.get('row_count', 0),\n                    'has_checksum': 'checksum' in backup_data\n                })\n        except:\n            pass\n        \n        return info\n        \n    except Exception:\n        return None\n\n\ndef format_size(size_bytes: int) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Format byte size to human readable.\"\"\"\n    if size_bytes < 1024:\n        return f\"{size_bytes} B\"\n    elif size_bytes < 1024 * 1024:\n        return f\"{size_bytes / 1024:.1f} KB\"\n    elif size_bytes < 1024 * 1024 * 1024:\n        return f\"{size_bytes / (1024 * 1024):.1f} MB\"\n    else:\n        return f\"{size_bytes / (1024 * 1024 * 1024):.1f} GB\"\n\n\nclass BackupManager:\n    \"\"\"Manage backup operations with integrity checking.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, backup_dir: str = './backups'):\n        self.backup_dir = backup_dir\n        os.makedirs(backup_dir, exist_ok=True)\n    \n    def create_backup(self, db_name: str, tables_data: Dict[str, Any],\n                     compression: str = 'gzip',\n                     compression_level: int = 6,\n                     passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create backup and save to file.\n        \n        Returns:\n            Path to backup file\n        \"\"\"\n        comp_type = CompressionType(compression) if compression else CompressionType.GZIP\n        \n        backup_data = create_backup(\n            db_name, tables_data,\n            compression=comp_type,\n            compression_level=compression_level,\n            passphrase=passphrase\n        )\n        \n        filename = generate_backup_filename(db_name, encrypted=passphrase is not None)\n        file_path = os.path.join(self.backup_dir, filename)\n        \n        with open(file_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(backup_data)\n        \n        return file_path\n    \n    def restore_backup(self, filename: str, passphrase: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Restore backup from file.\"\"\"\n        file_path = os.path.join(self.backup_dir, filename)\n        return restore_backup(file_path, passphrase)\n    \n    def list_backups(self) -> list:\n        \"\"\"List all backup files with info.\"\"\"\n        backups = []\n        for filename in os.listdir(self.backup_dir):\n            if filename.endswith('.json.gz') or filename.endswith('.enc'):\n                file_path = os.path.join(self.backup_dir, filename)\n                info = get_backup_info(file_path)\n                if info:\n                    backups.append(info)\n        return sorted(backups, key=lambda x: x['modified'], reverse=True)\n    \n    def cleanup_old_backups(self, keep_count: int = 10):\n        \"\"\"Remove old backups keeping only the most recent.\"\"\"\n        backups = self.list_backups()\n        if len(backups) > keep_count:\n            for backup in backups[keep_count:]:\n                try:\n                    os.remove(backup['path'])\n                except:\n                    pass\n\n\ndef generate_backup_filename(db_name: str, suffix: str = '',\n                            encrypted: bool = False) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Generate standardized backup filename.\"\"\"\n    timestamp = datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')\n    ext = '.enc' if encrypted else '.json.gz'\n    if suffix:\n        return f\"{db_name}_{suffix}_{timestamp}{ext}\"\n    return f\"{db_name}_{timestamp}{ext}\"\n\n\n# Backward compatibility\n__all__ = [\n    'BackupIntegrityError',\n    'BackupEncryptionError',\n    'CompressionType',\n    'BackupManager',\n    'calculate_checksum',\n    'verify_backup_integrity',\n    'create_backup',\n    'restore_backup',\n    'create_backup_metadata',\n    'add_integrity_check',\n    'validate_before_restore',\n    'get_backup_info',\n    'format_size',\n    'generate_backup_filename',\n    'compress_data',\n    'decompress_data',\n    'encrypt_data',\n    'decrypt_data'\n]\n</contentOfFile>\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 16, "timestamp": 1783714064.172288, "date": "2026-07-10", "thinking": "", "prompt_tokens": 86611, "response_tokens": 4347} -->

for SQL-like commands.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self):\n        # Initialize command patterns\n        self.patterns = {\n            'CREATE_DB': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*CREATE\\s+DATABASE\\s+(?P<db_name>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'DROP_DB': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*DROP\\s+DATABASE\\s+(?P<db_name>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'USE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*USE\\s+(?P<db_name>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'CREATE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*CREATE\\s+TABLE\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)\\s*\\((?P<columns>[^)]+)\\)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'DROP': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*DROP\\s+TABLE\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'ALTER': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*ALTER\\s+TABLE\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)\\s+(?P<alter_spec>.+)$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'INSERT': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*INSERT\\s+INTO\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)\\s+VALUES\\s*\\((?P<values>[^)]+)\\)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'SELECT': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*SELECT\\s+(?P<columns>[\\w\\s,]+)\\s+FROM\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)(?:\\s+WHERE\\s+(?P<where>.+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'UPDATE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*UPDATE\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)\\s+SET\\s+(?P<set>[\\w\\s=,]+)(?:\\s+WHERE\\s+(?P<where>.+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'DELETE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*DELETE\\s+FROM\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)(?:\\s+WHERE\\s+(?P<where>.+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'SHOW': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*SHOW\\s+(?P<target>DATABASES|TABLES|USERS)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'DESCRIBE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*DESCRIBE\\s+(?P<table>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'CREATE_USER': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*CREATE\\s+USER\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s+PASSWORD\\s+(?P<password>\\S+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'DROP_USER': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*DROP\\s+USER\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'GRANT': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*GRANT\\s+(?P<privileges>[\\w\\s,]+)\\s+ON\\s+(?P<db>\\w+)\\.(?P<table>\\w+)\\s+TO\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'REVOKE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*REVOKE\\s+(?P<privileges>[\\w\\s,]+)\\s+ON\\s+(?P<db>\\w+)\\.(?P<table>\\w+)\\s+FROM\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'CREATE_ROLE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*CREATE\\s+ROLE\\s+(?P<role_name>\\w+)(?:\\s+DESCRIPTION\\s+(?P<description>\\'[^\\']*\\'|\\\"[^\\\"]*\\\"))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'DROP_ROLE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*DROP\\s+ROLE\\s+(?P<role_name>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'GRANT_ROLE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*GRANT\\s+ROLE\\s+(?P<role_name>\\w+)\\s+TO\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'REVOKE_ROLE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*REVOKE\\s+ROLE\\s+(?P<role_name>\\w+)\\s+FROM\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'SHOW_GRANTS': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*SHOW\\s+GRANTS(?:\\s+FOR\\s+(?P<username>\\w+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'SHOW_ROLES': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*SHOW\\s+ROLES\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'AUDIT_LOG': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*AUDIT\\s+LOG(?:\\s+LIMIT\\s+(?P<limit>\\d+))?(?:\\s+USER\\s+(?P<user>\\w+))?(?:\\s+ACTION\\s+(?P<action>\\w+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n        }\n    \n    def parse(self, sql: str) -> Tuple[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse SQL-like command and return command type and parameters.\n        \n        Args:\n            sql: SQL command string\n        \n        Returns:\n            Tuple of (command_type, parameters_dict)\n        \"\"\"\n        sql = sql.strip()\n        \n        # Try each pattern\n        for cmd_type, pattern in self.patterns.items():\n            match = pattern.match(sql)\n            if match:\n                params = match.groupdict()\n                \n                # Parse special fields\n                if 'columns' in params and params['columns']:\n                    params['columns'] = [c.strip() for c in params['columns'].split(',')]\n                \n                if 'values' in params and params['values']:\n                    params['values'] = self._parse_values(params['values'])\n                \n                if 'privileges' in params and params['privileges']:\n                    params['privileges'] = [p.strip().upper() for p in params['privileges'].split(',')]\n                \n                if 'description' in params and params['description']:\n                    # Remove quotes\n                    params['description'] = params['description'].strip('\\'\"')\n                \n                if 'limit' in params and params['limit']:\n                    params['limit'] = int(params['limit'])\n                \n                return cmd_type, params\n        \n        # Check for simple commands\n        sql_upper = sql.upper()\n        if sql_upper == 'QUIT' or sql_upper == 'EXIT':\n            return 'QUIT', {}\n        \n        return 'UNKNOWN', {'sql': sql}\n    \n    def _parse_values(self, values_str: str) -> List[Any]:\n        \"\"\"Parse VALUES clause.\"\"\"\n        values = []\n        for val in values_str.split(','):\n            val = val.strip()\n            # Try to convert to number\n            try:\n                if '.' in val:\n                    values.append(float(val))\n                else:\n                    values.append(int(val))\n            except ValueError:\n                # Remove quotes if present\n                if (val.startswith(\"'\") and val.endswith(\"'\")) or \\\n                   (val.startswith('\"') and val.endswith('\"')):\n                    val = val[1:-1]\n                values.append(val)\n        return values\n\n\nclass BackupRestoreParser:\n    \"\"\"Parser for backup and restore commands with encryption and compression support.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self):\n        self.patterns = {\n            'BACKUP': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*BACKUP\\s+(?:DATABASE\\s+)?(?P<db_name>\\w+)' +\n                r'(?:\\s+TO\\s+(?P<backup_path>\\S+))?' +\n                r'(?:\\s+WITH\\s+(?P<options>.+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'RESTORE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*RESTORE\\s+(?:DATABASE\\s+)?(?P<db_name>\\w+)' +\n                r'(?:\\s+FROM\\s+(?P<backup_path>\\S+))?' +\n                r'(?:\\s+WITH\\s+(?P<options>.+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n        }\n    \n    def parse(self, sql: str) -> Tuple[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n        \"\"\"Parse backup/restore commands.\"\"\"\n        sql = sql.strip()\n        \n        for cmd_type, pattern in self.patterns.items():\n            match = pattern.match(sql)\n            if match:\n                params = match.groupdict()\n                \n                # Parse options (ENCRYPTION, COMPRESSION, etc.)\n                options_str = params.get('options', '')\n                if options_str:\n                    options = self._parse_options(options_str)\n                    params.update(options)\n                \n                return cmd_type, params\n        \n        # Fall back to regular parser\n        regular_parser = CommandParser()\n        return regular_parser.parse(sql)\n    \n    def _parse_options(self, options_str: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Parse WITH options for backup/restore.\"\"\"\n        options = {}\n        \n        # ENCRYPTION 'password'\n        enc_match = re.search(r\"ENCRYPTION\\s+['\\\"]([^'\\\"]+)['\\\"]\", options_str, re.IGNORECASE)\n        if enc_match:\n            options['encryption'] = enc_match.group(1)\n        \n        # COMPRESSION 'gzip|lz4|zstd|none'\n        comp_match = re.search(r\"COMPRESSION\\s+(\\w+)\", options_str, re.IGNORECASE)\n        if comp_match:\n            options['compression'] = comp_match.group(1).lower()\n        \n        # LEVEL 1-9\n        level_match = re.search(r\"LEVEL\\s+(\\d+)\", options_str, re.IGNORECASE)\n        if level_match:\n            options['compression_level'] = int(level_match.group(1))\n        \n        return options\n\n\n# Example usage\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    parser = CommandParser()\n    \n    test_commands = [\n        \"CREATE DATABASE testdb\",\n        \"USE testdb\",\n        \"CREATE TABLE users (id INT, name TEXT)\",\n        \"INSERT INTO users VALUES (1, 'Alice')\",\n        \"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = 1\",\n        \"UPDATE users SET name = 'Bob' WHERE id = 1\",\n        \"DELETE FROM users WHERE id = 1\",\n        \"DROP TABLE users\",\n        \"DROP DATABASE testdb\",\n        \"GRANT SELECT, INSERT ON testdb.users TO alice\",\n        \"REVOKE DELETE ON testdb.users FROM alice\",\n        \"CREATE ROLE readonly DESCRIPTION 'Read-only access'\",\n        \"GRANT ROLE readonly TO alice\",\n        \"SHOW GRANTS FOR alice\",\n        \"SHOW ROLES\",\n    ]\n    \n    for cmd in test_commands:\n        cmd_type, params = parser.parse(cmd)\n        print(f\"Command: {cmd}\")\n        print(f\"  Type: {cmd_type}\")\n        print(f\"  Params: {params}\")\n        print()\n    \n    # Test backup/restore parser\n    backup_parser = BackupRestoreParser()\n    backup_commands = [\n        \"BACKUP DATABASE mydb TO /path/backup WITH ENCRYPTION 'mypass' COMPRESSION gzip\",\n        \"BACKUP mydb WITH COMPRESSION lz4 LEVEL 9\",\n        \"RESTORE DATABASE mydb FROM /path/backup WITH ENCRYPTION 'mypass'\",\n        \"RESTORE mydb FROM backup.enc\",\n    ]\n    \n    print(\"=== Backup/Restore Commands ===\")\nn    for cmd in backup_commands:\\n        cmd_type, params = backup_parser.parse(cmd)\\n        print(f\"Command: {cmd}\")\\n        print(f\"  Type: {cmd_type}\")\\n        print(f\"  Params: {params}\")\\n        print()\\n</contentOfFile>\\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 16, "timestamp": 1783714087.4100614, "date": "2026-07-10", "thinking": "", r'^\\s*DROP\\s+USER\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'GRANT': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*GRANT\\s+(?P<privileges>[\\w\\s,]+)\\s+ON\\s+(?P<db>\\w+)\\.(?P<table>\\w+)\\s+TO\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'REVOKE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*REVOKE\\s+(?P<privileges>[\\w\\s,]+)\\s+ON\\s+(?P<db>\\w+)\\.(?P<table>\\w+)\\s+FROM\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'CREATE_ROLE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*CREATE\\s+ROLE\\s+(?P<role_name>\\w+)(?:\\s+DESCRIPTION\\s+(?P<description>\\'[^\\']*\\'|\\\"[^\\\"]*\\\"))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'DROP_ROLE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*DROP\\s+ROLE\\s+(?P<role_name>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'GRANT_ROLE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*GRANT\\s+ROLE\\s+(?P<role_name>\\w+)\\s+TO\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'REVOKE_ROLE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*REVOKE\\s+ROLE\\s+(?P<role_name>\\w+)\\s+FROM\\s+(?P<username>\\w+)\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'SHOW_GRANTS': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*SHOW\\s+GRANTS(?:\\s+FOR\\s+(?P<username>\\w+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'SHOW_ROLES': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*SHOW\\s+ROLES\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'AUDIT_LOG': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*AUDIT\\s+LOG(?:\\s+LIMIT\\s+(?P<limit>\\d+))?(?:\\s+USER\\s+(?P<user>\\w+))?(?:\\s+ACTION\\s+(?P<action>\\w+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n        }\n    \n    def parse(self, sql: str) -> Tuple[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse SQL-like command and return command type and parameters.\n        \n        Args:\n            sql: SQL command string\n        \n        Returns:\n            Tuple of (command_type, parameters_dict)\n        \"\"\"\n        sql = sql.strip()\n        \n        # Try each pattern\n        for cmd_type, pattern in self.patterns.items():\n            match = pattern.match(sql)\n            if match:\n                params = match.groupdict()\n                \n                # Parse special fields\n                if 'columns' in params and params['columns']:\n                    params['columns'] = [c.strip() for c in params['columns'].split(',')]\n                \n                if 'values' in params and params['values']:\n                    params['values'] = self._parse_values(params['values'])\n                \n                if 'privileges' in params and params['privileges']:\n                    params['privileges'] = [p.strip().upper() for p in params['privileges'].split(',')]\n                \n                if 'description' in params and params['description']:\n                    # Remove quotes\n                    params['description'] = params['description'].strip('\\'\"')\n                \n                if 'limit' in params and params['limit']:\n                    params['limit'] = int(params['limit'])\n                \n                return cmd_type, params\n        \n        # Check for simple commands\n        sql_upper = sql.upper()\n        if sql_upper == 'QUIT' or sql_upper == 'EXIT':\n            return 'QUIT', {}\n        \n        return 'UNKNOWN', {'sql': sql}\n    \n    def _parse_values(self, values_str: str) -> List[Any]:\n        \"\"\"Parse VALUES clause.\"\"\"\n        values = []\n        for val in values_str.split(','):\n            val = val.strip()\n            # Try to convert to number\n            try:\n                if '.' in val:\n                    values.append(float(val))\n                else:\n                    values.append(int(val))\n            except ValueError:\n                # Remove quotes if present\n                if (val.startswith(\"'\") and val.endswith(\"'\")) or \\\n                   (val.startswith('\"') and val.endswith('\"')):\n                    val = val[1:-1]\n                values.append(val)\n        return values\n\n\nclass BackupRestoreParser:\n    \"\"\"Parser for backup and restore commands with encryption and compression support.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self):\n        self.patterns = {\n            'BACKUP': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*BACKUP\\s+(?:DATABASE\\s+)?(?P<db_name>\\w+)' +\n                r'(?:\\s+TO\\s+(?P<backup_path>\\S+))?' +\n                r'(?:\\s+WITH\\s+(?P<options>.+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n            'RESTORE': re.compile(\n                r'^\\s*RESTORE\\s+(?:DATABASE\\s+)?(?P<db_name>\\w+)' +\n                r'(?:\\s+FROM\\s+(?P<backup_path>\\S+))?' +\n                r'(?:\\s+WITH\\s+(?P<options>.+))?\\s*$',\n                re.IGNORECASE\n            ),\n        }\n    \n    def parse(self, sql: str) -> Tuple[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n        \"\"\"Parse backup/restore commands.\"\"\"\n        sql = sql.strip()\n        \n        for cmd_type, pattern in self.patterns.items():\n            match = pattern.match(sql)\n            if match:\n                params = match.groupdict()\n                \n                # Parse options (ENCRYPTION, COMPRESSION, etc.)\n                options_str = params.get('options', '')\n                if options_str:\n                    options = self._parse_options(options_str)\n                    params.update(options)\n                \n                return cmd_type, params\n        \n        # Fall back to regular parser\n        regular_parser = CommandParser()\n        return regular_parser.parse(sql)\n    \n    def _parse_options(self, options_str: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Parse WITH options for backup/restore.\"\"\"\n        options = {}\n        \n        # ENCRYPTION 'password'\n        enc_match = re.search(r\"ENCRYPTION\\s+['\\\"]([^'\\\"]+)['\\\"]\", options_str, re.IGNORECASE)\n        if enc_match:\n            options['encryption'] = enc_match.group(1)\n        \n        # COMPRESSION 'gzip|lz4|zstd|none'\n        comp_match = re.search(r\"COMPRESSION\\s+(\\w+)\", options_str, re.IGNORECASE)\n        if comp_match:\n            options['compression'] = comp_match.group(1).lower()\n        \n        # LEVEL 1-9\n        level_match = re.search(r\"LEVEL\\s+(\\d+)\", options_str, re.IGNORECASE)\n        if level_match:\n            options['compression_level'] = int(level_match.group(1))\n        \n        return options\n\n\n# Example usage\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    parser = CommandParser()\n    \n    test_commands = [\n        \"CREATE DATABASE testdb\",\n        \"USE testdb\",\n        \"CREATE TABLE users (id INT, name TEXT)\",\n        \"INSERT INTO users VALUES (1, 'Alice')\",\n        \"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = 1\",\n        \"UPDATE users SET name = 'Bob' WHERE id = 1\",\n        \"DELETE FROM users WHERE id = 1\",\n        \"DROP TABLE users\",\n        \"DROP DATABASE testdb\",\n        \"GRANT SELECT, INSERT ON testdb.users TO alice\",\n        \"REVOKE DELETE ON testdb.users FROM alice\",\n        \"CREATE ROLE readonly DESCRIPTION 'Read-only access'\",\n        \"GRANT ROLE readonly TO alice\",\n        \"SHOW GRANTS FOR alice\",\n        \"SHOW ROLES\",\n    ]\n    \n    for cmd in test_commands:\n        cmd_type, params = parser.parse(cmd)\n        print(f\"Command: {cmd}\")\n        print(f\"  Type: {cmd_type}\")\n        print(f\"  Params: {params}\")\n        print()\n    \n    # Test backup/restore parser\n    backup_parser = BackupRestoreParser()\n    backup_commands = [\n        \"BACKUP DATABASE mydb TO /path/backup WITH ENCRYPTION 'mypass' COMPRESSION gzip\",\n        \"BACKUP mydb WITH COMPRESSION lz4 LEVEL 9\",\n        \"RESTORE DATABASE mydb FROM /path/backup WITH ENCRYPTION 'mypass'\",\n        \"RESTORE mydb FROM backup.enc\",\n    ]\n    \n    print(\"=== Backup/Restore Commands ===\")\n    for cmd in backup_commands:\n        cmd_type, params = backup_parser.parse(cmd)\n        print(f\"Command: {cmd}\")\n        print(f\"  Type: {cmd_type}\")\n        print(f\"  Params: {params}\")\n        print()\n</contentOfFile>\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 17, "timestamp": 1783714114.1651616, "date": "2026-07-10", "thinking": "", "prompt_tokens": 93800, "response_tokens": 2654} -->

<!-- {"role": "assistant", "content": "<ReplaceLine>\n<fileName>commands.py</fileName>\n<fromLine>230</fromLine>\n<toLine>270</toLine>\n<replacement>    def _backup(self, params, state):\n        db_name = params.get('db_name') or state.get('current_db')\n        backup_path = params.get('backup_path')\n        \n        # Parse encryption and compression options\n        passphrase = params.get('encryption')\n        compression = params.get('compression', 'gzip')\n        compression_level = params.get('compression_level', 6)\n        \n        if not db_name:\n            return \"ERROR: No database specified\"\n        \n        if not BACKUP_UTILS_AVAILABLE:\n            # Fallback to basic backup\n            return self.db.backup_database(db_name, backup_path)\n        \n        try:\n            # Get all tables data\n            tables_data = {}\n            tables = self.db._list_tables(db_name)\n            \n            for table_name in tables:\n                table_info = self.db._get_table(db_name, table_name)\n                if table_info:\n                    tables_data[table_name] = {\n                        'schema': table_info.get('schema', []),\n                        'rows': table_info.get('rows', [])\n                    }\n            \n            # Create backup with encryption and compression\n            comp_type = CompressionType(compression) if compression else CompressionType.GZIP\n            \n            backup_data = create_backup(\n                db_name=db_name,\n                tables_data=tables_data,\n                compression=comp_type,\n                compression_level=compression_level,\n                passphrase=passphrase\n            )\n            \n            # Determine backup path\n            if not backup_path:\n                timestamp = time.strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')\n                ext = '.enc' if passphrase else '.json.gz'\n                backup_path = f\"./backups/{db_name}_{timestamp}{ext}\"\n            \n            # Ensure directory exists\n            os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(backup_path) or '.', exist_ok=True)\n            \n            # Write backup file\n            with open(backup_path, 'wb') as f:\n                f.write(backup_data)\n            \n            # Verify integrity\n            valid, error = verify_backup_integrity(backup_path, passphrase)\n            if not valid:\n                return f\"ERROR: Backup integrity check failed: {error}\"\n            \n            return f\"OK: Backup created at {backup_path} ({len(backup_data)} bytes)\"\n            \n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Backup failed: {e}\")\n            return f\"ERROR: Backup failed: {e}\"\n    \n    def _restore(self, params, state):\n        db_name = params.get('db_name')\n        backup_path = params.get('backup_path')\n        passphrase = params.get('encryption')\n        \n        if not db_name:\n            return \"ERROR: RESTORE requires database name\"\n        \n        if not backup_path:\n            return \"ERROR: RESTORE requires backup path\"\n        \n        if not BACKUP_UTILS_AVAILABLE:\n            # Fallback to basic restore\n            return self.db.restore_database(db_name, backup_path)\n        \n        try:\n            # Verify integrity first\n            valid, error = verify_backup_integrity(backup_path, passphrase)\n            if not valid:\n                return f\"ERROR: Backup integrity check failed: {error}\"\n            \n            # Restore backup\n            backup_data = restore_backup(backup_path, passphrase)\n            \n            # Restore tables\n            tables = backup_data.get('tables', {})\n            restored_count = 0\n            \n            for table_name, table_info in tables.items():\n                # Create table if not exists\n                self.db.create_table(db_name, table_name, table_info.get('schema', []))\n                \n                # Insert rows\n                for row in table_info.get('rows', []):\n                    self.db.insert(db_name, table_name, row)\n                \n                restored_count += 1\n            \n            return f\"OK: Restored {restored_count} tables from {backup_path}\"\n            \n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Restore failed: {e}\")\n            return f\"ERROR: Restore failed: {e}\"</replacement>\n</ReplaceLine>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 17, "timestamp": 1783714128.7991834, "date": "2026-07-10", "thinking": "", "prompt_tokens": 96693, "response_tokens": 871} -->
