found: {self.config_path}\")\n        \n        try:\n            with open(self.config_path, 'r') as f:\n                raw_config = f.read()\n            \n            config = json.loads(raw_config)\n            \n            # Substitute environment variables\n            if substitute_env:\n                config = self._substitute_env_vars(config)\n            \n            # Validate configuration\n            if validate:\n                errors = self._validate_config(config)\n                if errors:\n                    raise ConfigError(f\"Configuration validation failed: \" + \"; \".join(errors))\n            \n            with self._lock:\n                self._config = config\n                self._last_loaded = time.time()\n                self._last_modified = self.config_path.stat().st_mtime\n            \n            logger.info(f\"Configuration loaded from {self.config_path}\")\n            return config\n            \n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            raise ConfigError(f\"Invalid JSON in configuration file: {e}\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            raise ConfigError(f\"Failed to load configuration: {e}\")\n    \n    def _substitute_env_vars(self, config: Any) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"\n        Recursively substitute environment variables in configuration.\n        \n        Supports ${VAR_NAME} or $VAR_NAME syntax.\n        \"\"\"\n        import re\n        \n        if isinstance(config, dict):\n            return {k: self._substitute_env_vars(v) for k, v in config.items()}\n        elif isinstance(config, list):\n            return [self._substitute_env_vars(item) for item in config]\n        elif isinstance(config, str):\n            # Pattern: ${VAR_NAME} or $VAR_NAME\n            pattern = re.compile(r'\\$\\{(\\w+)\\}|\\$(\\w+)')\n            \n            def replace_env_var(match):\n                var_name = match.group(1) or match.group(2)\n                value = os.getenv(var_name)\n                if value is None:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Environment variable {var_name} not set\")\n                    return match.group(0)  # Keep original\n                return value\n            \n            return pattern.sub(replace_env_var, config)\n        else:\n            return config\n    \n    def _validate_config(self, config: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate configuration against schema.\n        \n        Returns:\n            List of validation errors (empty if valid)\n        \"\"\"\n        errors = []\n        \n        # Check required sections\n        required_sections = ['server', 'database']\n        for section in required_sections:\n            if section not in config:\n                errors.append(f\"Missing required section: {section}\")\n        \n        # Basic validation\n        if 'server' in config:\n            server = config['server']\n            if 'port' in server and not (1 <= server['port'] <= 65535):\n                errors.append(\"server.port must be between 1 and 65535\")\n        \n        if 'database' in config:\n            db = config['database']\n            if 'encryption' in db and db['encryption'].get('enabled'):\n                if not db['encryption'].get('key_file') and not db['encryption'].get('passphrase_env'):\n                    errors.append(\"database.encryption requires key_file or passphrase_env when enabled\")\n        \n        # Cross-field validation\n        if 'performance' in config:\n            perf = config['performance']\n            if 'connection_pool_min' in perf and 'connection_pool_max' in perf:\n                if perf['connection_pool_min'] > perf['connection_pool_max']:\n                    errors.append(\"performance.connection_pool_min cannot be greater than connection_pool_max\")\n        \n        return errors\n    \n    def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get configuration value by dot-notation key.\n        \n        Args:\n            key: Dot-notation key (e.g., 'server.port')\n            default: Default value if key not found\n        \n        Returns:\n            Configuration value\n        \"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            keys = key.split('.')\n            value = self._config\n            \n            for k in keys:\n                if isinstance(value, dict) and k in value:\n                    value = value[k]\n                else:\n                    return default\n            \n            return value\n    \n    def get_all(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Get full configuration.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            return self._config.copy()\n    \n    def reload(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Reload configuration if file has changed.\n        \n        Returns:\n            True if reloaded, False if unchanged\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self.config_path.exists():\n            logger.warning(f\"Configuration file not found: {self.config_path}\")\n            return False\n        \n        current_mtime = self.config_path.stat().st_mtime\n        \n        with self._lock:\n            if current_mtime <= self._last_modified:\n                return False\n            \n            try:\n                old_config = self._config.copy()\n                self.load(validate=True, substitute_env=True)\n                \n                # Notify callbacks\n                changes = self._detect_changes(old_config, self._config)\n                if changes:\n                    logger.info(f\"Configuration reloaded. Changes: {changes}\")\n                    for callback in self._reload_callbacks:\n                        try:\n                            callback(changes)\n                        except Exception as e:\n                            logger.error(f\"Reload callback failed: {e}\")\n                \n                return True\n                \n            except ConfigError as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Failed to reload configuration: {e}\")\n                return False\n    \n    def _detect_changes(self, old: Dict, new: Dict, prefix: str = '') -> List[str]:\n        \"\"\"Detect changes between old and new configuration.\"\"\"\n        changes = []\n        \n        all_keys = set(old.keys()) | set(new.keys())\n        \n        for key in all_keys:\n            full_key = f\"{prefix}.{key}\" if prefix else key\n            \n            if key not in old:\n                changes.append(f\"+{full_key}\")\n            elif key not in new:\n                changes.append(f\"-{full_key}\")\n            elif isinstance(old[key], dict) and isinstance(new[key], dict):\n                changes.extend(self._detect_changes(old[key], new[key], full_key))\n            elif old[key] != new[key]:\n                # Mask sensitive values\n                if any(s in full_key.lower() for s in self.SENSITIVE_KEYS):\n                    changes.append(f\"~{full_key}: ***\")\n                else:\n                    changes.append(f\"~{full_key}: {old[key]} -> {new[key]}\")\n        \n        return changes\n    \n    def start_hot_reload(self, interval: int = 30):\n        \"\"\"\n        Start hot-reload watcher thread.\n        \n        Args:\n            interval: Check interval in seconds\n        \"\"\"\n        if self._reload_thread and self._reload_thread.is_alive():\n            logger.warning(\"Hot-reload already running\")\n            return\n        \n        self._stop_reload.clear()\n        \n        def watcher():\n            while not self._stop_reload.wait(interval):\n                try:\n                    if self.reload():\n                        logger.debug(\"Configuration hot-reloaded\")\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.error(f\"Hot-reload error: {e}\")\n        \n        self._reload_thread = threading.Thread(target=watcher, daemon=True)\n        self._reload_thread.start()\n        logger.info(f\"Hot-reload started (interval={interval}s)\")\n    \n    def stop_hot_reload(self):\n        \"\"\"Stop hot-reload watcher.\"\"\"\n        self._stop_reload.set()\n        if self._reload_thread:\n            self._reload_thread.join(timeout=5)\n            logger.info(\"Hot-reload stopped\")\n    \n    def on_reload(self, callback: Callable[[List[str]], None]):\n        \"\"\"\n        Register callback for configuration reload.\n        \n        Args:\n            callback: Function accepting list of changes\n        \"\"\"\n        self._reload_callbacks.append(callback)\n    \n    def get_sensitive_value(self, env_var: str) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get sensitive value from environment variable.\n        \n        Args:\n            env_var: Environment variable name\n        \n        Returns:\n            Value or None if not set\n        \"\"\"\n        return os.getenv(env_var)\n    \n    def mask_config(self, config: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create masked copy of configuration for logging.\n        \n        Args:\n            config: Configuration dictionary\n        \n        Returns:\n            Masked configuration\n        \"\"\"\n        masked = {}\n        \n        for key, value in config.items():\n            if any(s in key.lower() for s in self.SENSITIVE_KEYS):\n                masked[key] = '***'\n            elif isinstance(value, dict):\n                masked[key] = self.mask_config(value)\n            elif isinstance(value, list):\n                masked[key] = [\n                    self.mask_config(item) if isinstance(item, dict) else item\n                    for item in value\n                ]\n            else:\n                masked[key] = value\n        \n        return masked\n    \n    def print_config(self, mask_sensitive: bool = True):\n        \"\"\"Print current configuration.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\nn            config = self._config\\n            if mask_sensitive:\\n                config = self.mask_config(config)\\n        \\n        print(\"=\" * 60)\\n        print(\"Current Configuration:\")\\n        print(\"=\" * 60)\\n        print(json.dumps(config, indent=2))\\n        print(\"=\" * 60)\\n\\n\\ndef load_config(config_path: str = 'config.json',\\n                validate: bool = True,\\n                substitute_env: bool = True) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Convenience function to load configuration.\\n    \\n    Args:\\n        config_path: Path to configuration file\\n        validate: Whether to validate\\n        substitute_env: Whether to substitute environment variables\\n    \\n    Returns:\\n        Configuration dictionary\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    loader = ConfigLoader(config_path)\\n    return loader.load(validate=validate, substitute_env=substitute_env)\\n\\n\\n# Example usage\\nif __name__ == '__main__':\\n    # Create sample config\\n    sample_config = {\\n        \"version\": \"3.1.0\",\\n        \"server\": {\\n            \"host\": \"0.0.0.0\",\\n            \"port\": 9999,\\n            \"data_dir\": \"./data\"\\n        },\\n        \"database\": {\\n            \"encryption\": {\\n                \"enabled\": True,\\n                \"passphrase_env\": \"KOSDB_SECRET\"\\n            }\\n        }\\n    }\\n    \\n    # Save sample\\n    with open('config.json', 'w') as f:\\n        json.dump(sample_config, f, indent=2)\\n    \\n    # Load with env substitution\\n    os.environ['KOSDB_SECRET'] = 'my-secret-key'\\n    \\n    loader = ConfigLoader('config.json')\\n    config = loader.load()\\n    \\n    print(\"Loaded configuration:\")\\n    loader.print_config()\\n</contentOfFile>\\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 19, "timestamp": 1783714463.8291829, "date": "2026-07-10", "thinking": "", "prompt_tokens": 139922, Sensitive keys that should be masked in logs\n    SENSITIVE_KEYS = {\n        'password', 'secret', 'key', 'token', 'passphrase', 'private_key',\n        'encryption_key', 'jwt_secret', 'api_key'\n    }\n    \n    def __init__(self, config_path: str = 'config.json'):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize configuration loader.\n        \n        Args:\n            config_path: Path to configuration file\n        \"\"\"\n        self.config_path = Path(config_path)\n        self._config: Dict[str, Any] = {}\n        self._last_modified: float = 0\n        self._last_loaded: float = 0\n        self._reload_callbacks: List[Callable] = []\n        self._reload_thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = None\n        self._stop_reload = threading.Event()\n        self._lock = threading.RLock()\n    \n    def load(self, validate: bool = True, substitute_env: bool = True) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load configuration from file.\n        \n        Args:\n            validate: Whether to validate configuration\n            substitute_env: Whether to substitute environment variables\n        \n        Returns:\n            Configuration dictionary\n        \n        Raises:\n            ConfigError: If configuration is invalid or file not found\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self.config_path.exists():\n            raise ConfigError(f\"Configuration file not found: {self.config_path}\")\n        \n        try:\n            with open(self.config_path, 'r') as f:\n                raw_config = f.read()\n            \n            config = json.loads(raw_config)\n            \n            # Substitute environment variables\n            if substitute_env:\n                config = self._substitute_env_vars(config)\n            \n            # Validate configuration\n            if validate:\n                errors = self._validate_config(config)\n                if errors:\n                    raise ConfigError(f\"Configuration validation failed: \" + \"; \".join(errors))\n            \n            with self._lock:\n                self._config = config\n                self._last_loaded = time.time()\n                self._last_modified = self.config_path.stat().st_mtime\n            \n            logger.info(f\"Configuration loaded from {self.config_path}\")\n            return config\n            \n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            raise ConfigError(f\"Invalid JSON in configuration file: {e}\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            raise ConfigError(f\"Failed to load configuration: {e}\")\n    \n    def _substitute_env_vars(self, config: Any) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"\n        Recursively substitute environment variables in configuration.\n        \n        Supports ${VAR_NAME} or $VAR_NAME syntax.\n        \"\"\"\n        import re\n        \n        if isinstance(config, dict):\n            return {k: self._substitute_env_vars(v) for k, v in config.items()}\n        elif isinstance(config, list):\n            return [self._substitute_env_vars(item) for item in config]\n        elif isinstance(config, str):\n            # Pattern: ${VAR_NAME} or $VAR_NAME\n            pattern = re.compile(r'\\$\\{(\\w+)\\}|\\$(\\w+)')\n            \n            def replace_env_var(match):\n                var_name = match.group(1) or match.group(2)\n                value = os.getenv(var_name)\n                if value is None:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Environment variable {var_name} not set\")\n                    return match.group(0)  # Keep original\n                return value\n            \n            return pattern.sub(replace_env_var, config)\n        else:\n            return config\n    \n    def _validate_config(self, config: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate configuration against schema.\n        \n        Returns:\n            List of validation errors (empty if valid)\n        \"\"\"\n        errors = []\n        \n        # Check required sections\n        required_sections = ['server', 'database']\n        for section in required_sections:\n            if section not in config:\n                errors.append(f\"Missing required section: {section}\")\n        \n        # Basic validation\n        if 'server' in config:\n            server = config['server']\n            if 'port' in server and not (1 <= server['port'] <= 65535):\n                errors.append(\"server.port must be between 1 and 65535\")\n        \n        if 'database' in config:\n            db = config['database']\n            if 'encryption' in db and db['encryption'].get('enabled'):\n                if not db['encryption'].get('key_file') and not db['encryption'].get('passphrase_env'):\n                    errors.append(\"database.encryption requires key_file or passphrase_env when enabled\")\n        \n        # Cross-field validation\n        if 'performance' in config:\n            perf = config['performance']\n            if 'connection_pool_min' in perf and 'connection_pool_max' in perf:\n                if perf['connection_pool_min'] > perf['connection_pool_max']:\n                    errors.append(\"performance.connection_pool_min cannot be greater than connection_pool_max\")\n        \n        return errors\n    \n    def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get configuration value by dot-notation key.\n        \n        Args:\n            key: Dot-notation key (e.g., 'server.port')\n            default: Default value if key not found\n        \n        Returns:\n            Configuration value\n        \"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            keys = key.split('.')\n            value = self._config\n            \n            for k in keys:\n                if isinstance(value, dict) and k in value:\n                    value = value[k]\n                else:\n                    return default\n            \n            return value\n    \n    def get_all(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Get full configuration.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            return self._config.copy()\n    \n    def reload(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Reload configuration if file has changed.\n        \n        Returns:\n            True if reloaded, False if unchanged\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self.config_path.exists():\n            logger.warning(f\"Configuration file not found: {self.config_path}\")\n            return False\n        \n        current_mtime = self.config_path.stat().st_mtime\n        \n        with self._lock:\n            if current_mtime <= self._last_modified:\n                return False\n            \n            try:\n                old_config = self._config.copy()\n                self.load(validate=True, substitute_env=True)\n                \n                # Notify callbacks\n                changes = self._detect_changes(old_config, self._config)\n                if changes:\n                    logger.info(f\"Configuration reloaded. Changes: {changes}\")\n                    for callback in self._reload_callbacks:\n                        try:\n                            callback(changes)\n                        except Exception as e:\n                            logger.error(f\"Reload callback failed: {e}\")\n                \n                return True\n                \n            except ConfigError as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Failed to reload configuration: {e}\")\n                return False\n    \n    def _detect_changes(self, old: Dict, new: Dict, prefix: str = '') -> List[str]:\n        \"\"\"Detect changes between old and new configuration.\"\"\"\n        changes = []\n        \n        all_keys = set(old.keys()) | set(new.keys())\n        \n        for key in all_keys:\n            full_key = f\"{prefix}.{key}\" if prefix else key\n            \n            if key not in old:\n                changes.append(f\"+{full_key}\")\n            elif key not in new:\n                changes.append(f\"-{full_key}\")\n            elif isinstance(old[key], dict) and isinstance(new[key], dict):\n                changes.extend(self._detect_changes(old[key], new[key], full_key))\n            elif old[key] != new[key]:\n                # Mask sensitive values\n                if any(s in full_key.lower() for s in self.SENSITIVE_KEYS):\n                    changes.append(f\"~{full_key}: ***\")\n                else:\n                    changes.append(f\"~{full_key}: {old[key]} -> {new[key]}\")\n        \n        return changes\n    \n    def start_hot_reload(self, interval: int = 30):\n        \"\"\"\n        Start hot-reload watcher thread.\n        \n        Args:\n            interval: Check interval in seconds\n        \"\"\"\n        if self._reload_thread and self._reload_thread.is_alive():\n            logger.warning(\"Hot-reload already running\")\n            return\n        \n        self._stop_reload.clear()\n        \n        def watcher():\n            while not self._stop_reload.wait(interval):\n                try:\n                    if self.reload():\n                        logger.debug(\"Configuration hot-reloaded\")\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.error(f\"Hot-reload error: {e}\")\n        \n        self._reload_thread = threading.Thread(target=watcher, daemon=True)\n        self._reload_thread.start()\n        logger.info(f\"Hot-reload started (interval={interval}s)\")\n    \n    def stop_hot_reload(self):\n        \"\"\"Stop hot-reload watcher.\"\"\"\n        self._stop_reload.set()\n        if self._reload_thread:\n            self._reload_thread.join(timeout=5)\n            logger.info(\"Hot-reload stopped\")\n    \n    def on_reload(self, callback: Callable[[List[str]], None]):\n        \"\"\"\n        Register callback for configuration reload.\n        \n        Args:\n            callback: Function accepting list of changes\n        \"\"\"\n        self._reload_callbacks.append(callback)\n    \n    def get_sensitive_value(self, env_var: str) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get sensitive value from environment variable.\n        \n        Args:\n            env_var: Environment variable name\n        \n        Returns:\n            Value or None if not set\n        \"\"\"\n        return os.getenv(env_var)\n    \n    def mask_config(self, config: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create masked copy of configuration for logging.\n        \n        Args:\n            config: Configuration dictionary\n        \n        Returns:\n            Masked configuration\n        \"\"\"\n        masked = {}\n        \n        for key, value in config.items():\n            if any(s in key.lower() for s in self.SENSITIVE_KEYS):\n                masked[key] = '***'\n            elif isinstance(value, dict):\n                masked[key] = self.mask_config(value)\n            elif isinstance(value, list):\n                masked[key] = [\n                    self.mask_config(item) if isinstance(item, dict) else item\n                    for item in value\n                ]\n            else:\n                masked[key] = value\n        \n        return masked\n    \n    def print_config(self, mask_sensitive: bool = True):\n        \"\"\"Print current configuration.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            config = self._config\n            if mask_sensitive:\n                config = self.mask_config(config)\n        \n        print(\"=\" * 60)\n        print(\"Current Configuration:\")\n        print(\"=\" * 60)\n        print(json.dumps(config, indent=2))\n        print(\"=\" * 60)\n\n\ndef load_config(config_path: str = 'config.json',\n                validate: bool = True,\n                substitute_env: bool = True) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience function to load configuration.\n    \n    Args:\n        config_path: Path to configuration file\n        validate: Whether to validate\n        substitute_env: Whether to substitute environment variables\n    \n    Returns:\n        Configuration dictionary\n    \"\"\"\n    loader = ConfigLoader(config_path)\n    return loader.load(validate=validate, substitute_env=substitute_env)\n\n\n# Example usage\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    # Create sample config\n    sample_config = {\n        \"version\": \"3.1.0\",\n        \"server\": {\n            \"host\": \"0.0.0.0\",\n            \"port\": 9999,\n            \"data_dir\": \"./data\"\n        },\n        \"database\": {\n            \"encryption\": {\n                \"enabled\": True,\n                \"passphrase_env\": \"KOSDB_SECRET\"\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    \n    # Save sample\n    with open('config.json', 'w') as f:\n        json.dump(sample_config, f, indent=2)\n    \n    # Load with env substitution\n    os.environ['KOSDB_SECRET'] = 'my-secret-key'\n    \n    loader = ConfigLoader('config.json')\n    config = loader.load()\n    \n    print(\"Loaded configuration:\")\n    loader.print_config()\n</contentOfFile>\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 19, Let me create comprehensive documentation covering all new features.\n\n<WriteFile>\n<fileName>README.md</fileName>\n<contentOfFile># KosDB v3.1.0 - High-Performance Database with Advanced Security\n\nA high-performance LevelDB-based database with SQL-like interface, featuring TLS encryption, GPU acceleration, comprehensive audit logging, and advanced replication.\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\n# Install dependencies\npip install plyvel cryptography\n\n# Start server\npython server.py\n\n# Connect with CLI\npython cli.py -u admin -p yourpassword\n```\n\n## New in v3.1.0\n\n### \ud83d\udd12 TLS/SSL Encryption\n- Secure client-server communication\n- Certificate validation\n- Self-signed certificate generation\n- Client certificate authentication\n\n### \ud83d\ude80 GPU Acceleration\n- CUDA-accelerated query processing\n- Vector operations on GPU\n- Configurable memory allocation\n- Automatic fallback to CPU\n\n### \ud83d\udccb Audit Logging\n- Complete operation audit trail\n- Multiple output targets (file, syslog, webhook)\n- Automatic log rotation\n- Sensitive data masking\n\n### \ud83d\udd10 Enhanced Security\n- AES-256-GCM at-rest encryption\n- Role-based access control (RBAC)\n- Column-level permissions\n- PBKDF2 key derivation\n\n## Installation\n\n### Requirements\n\n```bash\n# Core dependencies\npip install plyvel cryptography\n\n# Optional: GPU support\npip install pycuda\n\n# Optional: Advanced compression\npip install lz4 zstandard\n\n# Optional: Audit webhook\npip install requests\n```\n\n### From Source\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/yourorg/kosdb.git\ncd kosdb\npip install -e .\n```\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Basic Configuration\n\nCreate `config.json`:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"version\": \"3.1.0\",\n  \"server\": {\n    \"host\": \"0.0.0.0\",\n    \"port\": 9999,\n    \"data_dir\": \"./data\"\n  },\n  \"database\": {\n    \"engine\": \"leveldb\",\n    \"cache_size_mb\": 64\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### TLS Configuration\n\n```json\n{\n  \"tls\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"cert_file\": \"/path/to/server.crt\",\n    \"key_file\": \"/path/to/server.key\",\n    \"ca_file\": \"/path/to/ca.crt\",\n    \"client_auth\": true\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### GPU Configuration\n\n```json\n{\n  \"gpu\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"device_id\": 0,\n    \"memory_fraction\": 0.8,\n    \"kernels\": [\"vector_ops\", \"matrix_mult\", \"sort\"]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Audit Logging Configuration\n\n```json\n{\n  \"audit_logging\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"log_dir\": \"./audit_logs\",\n    \"max_size_mb\": 100,\n    \"max_age_days\": 30,\n    \"targets\": [\"file\", \"syslog\"],\n    \"exclude_commands\": [\"PING\", \"ECHO\"],\n    \"mask_commands\": [\"PASS\", \"PASSWORD\"]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Usage\n\n### Starting the Server\n\n```bash\n# Basic start\npython server.py\n\n# With configuration file\npython server.py -c config.json\n\n# With TLS\npython server.py --tls-cert server.crt --tls-key server.key\n\n# With encryption\nexport KOSDB_ENCRYPTION_PASSPHRASE=\"your-secret-key\"\npython server.py\n```\n\n### CLI Client\n\n```bash\n# Basic connection\npython cli.py -H localhost -p 9999 -u admin -P password\n\n# With TLS\npython cli.py --tls --ca-cert ca.crt -u admin\n\n# Execute script\npython cli.py -f script.sql\n\n# Single command\npython cli.py -c \"SELECT * FROM users\"\n```\n\n### SQL Commands\n\n```sql\n-- Database operations\nCREATE DATABASE mydb;\nUSE mydb;\nDROP DATABASE mydb;\n\n-- Table operations\nCREATE TABLE users (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, email TEXT);\nDESCRIBE users;\nDROP TABLE users;\n\n-- Data operations\nINSERT INTO users VALUES (1, 'Alice', 'alice@example.com');\nSELECT * FROM users WHERE id = 1;\nUPDATE users SET name = 'Bob' WHERE id = 1;\nDELETE FROM users WHERE id = 1;\n\n-- User management\nCREATE USER alice PASSWORD 'secret123';\nGRANT SELECT, INSERT ON mydb.users TO alice;\nREVOKE DELETE ON mydb.users FROM alice;\nSHOW GRANTS FOR alice;\n\n-- Role management\nCREATE ROLE readonly DESCRIPTION 'Read-only access';\nGRANT ROLE readonly TO alice;\nSHOW ROLES;\n\n-- Backup with encryption\nBACKUP DATABASE mydb TO /backups/mydb.backup WITH ENCRYPTION 'backup-key' COMPRESSION gzip LEVEL 9;\n\n-- Restore from encrypted backup\nRESTORE DATABASE mydb FROM /backups/mydb.backup WITH ENCRYPTION 'backup-key';\n\n-- Audit log query (admin only)\nAUDIT LOG LIMIT 100 USER alice ACTION DATA_WRITE;\n```\n\n## Security Features\n\n### TLS Setup\n\n1. Generate certificates:\n```bash\n# Self-signed (development)\npython server.py --tls-generate-self-signed\n\n# Production with CA\nopenssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:4096 -keyout server.key -out server.crt -days 365\n```\n\n2. Configure client:\n```bash\npython cli.py --tls --ca-cert ca.crt --cert client.crt --key client.key\n```\n\n### Encryption at Rest\n\n```bash\n# Set encryption passphrase\nexport KOSDB_ENCRYPTION_PASSPHRASE=\"strong-passphrase-32-chars-long\"\n\n# Start server with encryption\npython server.py\n```\n\n### Role-Based Access Control\n\n```sql\n-- Create roles\nCREATE ROLE admin DESCRIPTION 'Full database access';\nCREATE ROLE developer DESCRIPTION 'Read-write access';\nCREATE ROLE analyst DESCRIPTION 'Read-only access';\n\n-- Grant privileges to roles\nGRANT ALL ON *.* TO admin;\nGRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE ON mydb.* TO developer;\nGRANT SELECT ON mydb.* TO analyst;\n\n-- Assign roles to users\nGRANT ROLE developer TO alice;\nGRANT ROLE analyst TO bob;\n```\n\n## Replication\n\n### Master-Slave Setup\n\n**Master (server_id=1):**\n```json\n{\n  \"replication\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"role\": \"master\",\n    \"replication_port\": 9998\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Slave (server_id=2):**\n```json\n{\n  \"replication\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"role\": \"slave\",\n    \"master_host\": \"master.example.com:9998\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Master-Master Setup\n\n```json\n{\n  \"replication\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"role\": \"master\",\n    \"replication_port\": 9998,\n    \"peer_host\": \"peer.example.com:9998\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Performance Tuning\n\n### Connection Pooling\n\n```json\n{\n  \"performance\": {\n    \"connection_pool_min\": 5,\n    \"connection_pool_max\": 20,\n    \"query_cache_size\": 1000\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### GPU Acceleration\n\nEnable GPU for vector operations:\n\n```sql\n-- GPU-accelerated aggregation (automatic)\nSELECT SUM(price), AVG(quantity) FROM orders;\n\n-- Force GPU execution\nSELECT /*+ GPU */ matrix_mult(A, B) FROM matrices;\n```\n\n## Monitoring\n\n### Prometheus Metrics\n\n```json\n{\n  \"monitoring\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"metrics_port\": 9090,\n    \"prometheus_enabled\": true\n  }\n}\n```\n\nAccess metrics at `http://localhost:9090/metrics`\n\n### Health Checks\n\n```bash\n# Server health\ncurl http://localhost:9090/health\n\n# Replication status\ncurl http://localhost:9090/replication/status\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### TLS Connection Issues\n\n```bash\n# Verify certificate\nopenssl x509 -in server.crt -text -noout\n\n# Test connection\nopenssl s_client -connect localhost:9999 -CAfile ca.crt\n```\n\n### GPU Not Available\n\n```bash\n# Check CUDA\nnvidia-smi\n\n# Verify pycuda\npython -c \"import pycuda.driver as drv; drv.init()\"\n```\n\n### Audit Log Issues\n\n```bash\n# Check permissions\nls -la audit_logs/\n\n# Verify syslog\nsudo tail -f /var/log/syslog | grep kosdb\n```\n\n## API Reference\n\nSee [API.md](API.md) for complete API documentation.\n\n## Migration\n\nSee [MIGRATION_v3.0_to_v3.1.md](MIGRATION_v3.0_to_v3.1.md) for upgrade instructions.\n\n## Security Guide\n\nSee [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md) for security hardening best practices.\n\n## Changelog\n\nSee [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) for version history.\n\n## License\n\nMIT License - See LICENSE file for details.\n\n## Support\n\n- GitHub Issues: https://github.com/yourorg/kosdb/issues\n- Documentation: https://kosdb.readthedocs.io\n- Community: https://discord.gg/kosdb\n</contentOfFile>\n</WriteFile>", "prompt_tokens": 147548, "response_tokens": 1921} -->

> <contentOfFile># KosDB v3.1.0
> 
> A high-performance, feature-rich LevelDB-based database server with SQL-like interface, replication, TLS encryption, GPU acceleration, and comprehensive audit logging.
> 
> ## Features
> 
> ### Core Database
> - **SQL-like Interface**: Familiar CREATE, INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE commands
> - **Multiple Databases**: Support for multiple named databases...

<!-- {"role": "assistant", "content": "I'll update the documentation for the v3.1.0 release with all the new features.\n\n<WriteFile>\n<fileName>README.md</fileName>\n<contentOfFile># KosDB v3.1.0\n\nA high-performance, feature-rich LevelDB-based database server with SQL-like interface, replication, TLS encryption, GPU acceleration, and comprehensive audit logging.\n\n## Features\n\n### Core Database\n- **SQL-like Interface**: Familiar CREATE, INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE commands\n- **Multiple Databases**: Support for multiple named databases\n- **Table Schema**: Define columns with types (INT, TEXT, FLOAT, etc.)\n- **Transactions**: ACID-compliant operations\n- **Indexing**: Primary and secondary index support\n\n### Security (New in v3.1.0)\n- **TLS/SSL Encryption**: Secure client-server communication\n  - Certificate-based authentication\n  - Self-signed certificate generation\n  - Client certificate verification (mTLS)\n- **At-Rest Encryption**: AES-256-GCM database encryption\n  - PBKDF2 key derivation\n  - Master key rotation support\n- **Audit Logging**: Comprehensive operation logging\n  - Multiple output targets (file, syslog, webhook)\n  - Log rotation and compression\n  - Sensitive command masking\n\n### Authentication & Authorization\n- **Database-backed Authentication**: Username/password with bcrypt hashing\n- **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)**: Create roles and assign to users\n- **Granular Permissions**: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, DROP, ALTER, INDEX, ADMIN\n- **Column-level Security**: Restrict access to specific columns\n- **Session Management**: JWT-based sessions with TTL\n\n### Replication & High Availability\n- **Master-Slave Replication**: Automatic failover support\n- **Master-Master Replication**: Multi-master with conflict resolution\n- **Async Replication**: Non-blocking replication\n- **Replication Lag Monitoring**: Track replication health\n\n### Performance Features\n- **GPU Acceleration**: CUDA-powered query processing\n  - Vector operations\n  - Matrix multiplication\n  - Sorting algorithms\n- **Connection Pooling**: Efficient connection management\n- **Query Caching**: Result cache for frequent queries\n- **Compression**: Multiple algorithms (gzip, lz4, zstd)\n\n### Backup & Recovery\n- **Hot Backups**: Non-blocking backup operations\n- **Encrypted Backups**: Password-protected backup files\n- **Compression Options**: gzip, lz4, zstd\n- **Point-in-Time Recovery**: Restore to specific timestamp\n- **Integrity Verification**: SHA-256 checksums\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Installation\n\n```bash\n# Clone repository\ngit clone https://github.com/yourusername/kosdb.git\ncd kosdb\n\n# Install dependencies\npip install -r requirements.txt\n\n# Optional: Install GPU support\npip install pycuda  # For CUDA acceleration\n\n# Optional: Install encryption\npip install cryptography  # For TLS and at-rest encryption\n```\n\n### Basic Usage\n\n```bash\n# Start server with default config\npython server.py\n\n# Or specify configuration file\npython server.py -c config.json\n\n# Create admin user\npython server.py --prepare_admin admin --prepare_password secret123\n```\n\n### Client Connection\n\n```bash\n# Connect with CLI client\npython cli.py -H localhost -p 9999 -u admin -P secret123\n\n# Or use with TLS\npython cli.py -H localhost -p 9999 --tls --ca-cert ca.crt -u admin -P secret123\n```\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Basic Configuration (config.json)\n\n```json\n{\n  \"version\": \"3.1.0\",\n  \"server\": {\n    \"host\": \"0.0.0.0\",\n    \"port\": 9999,\n    \"data_dir\": \"./data\"\n  },\n  \"database\": {\n    \"engine\": \"leveldb\",\n    \"compression\": true\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### TLS Configuration\n\n```json\n{\n  \"tls\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"cert_file\": \"/etc/kosdb/server.crt\",\n    \"key_file\": \"/etc/kosdb/server.key\",\n    \"ca_file\": \"/etc/kosdb/ca.crt\",\n    \"client_auth\": true\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Audit Logging Configuration\n\n```json\n{\n  \"audit_logging\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"log_dir\": \"./audit_logs\",\n    \"max_size_mb\": 100,\n    \"max_age_days\": 30,\n    \"targets\": [\"file\", \"syslog\"],\n    \"exclude_commands\": [\"PING\"],\n    \"mask_commands\": [\"PASS\", \"PASSWORD\"]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### GPU Configuration\n\n```json\n{\n  \"gpu\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"device_id\": 0,\n    \"memory_fraction\": 0.8,\n    \"kernels\": [\"vector_ops\", \"matrix_mult\", \"sort\"]\n  }\n}\n```\n\nSee [Configuration Guide](README_CONFIG.md) for complete documentation.\n\n## SQL Commands\n\n### Database Operations\n\n```sql\n-- Create database\nCREATE DATABASE mydb;\n\n-- Use database\nUSE mydb;\n\n-- Drop database\nDROP DATABASE mydb;\n```\n\n### Table Operations\n\n```sql\n-- Create table\nCREATE TABLE users (\n    id INT PRIMARY KEY,\n    name TEXT,\n    email TEXT,\n    age INT\n);\n\n-- Describe table\nDESCRIBE users;\n\n-- Drop table\nDROP TABLE users;\n```\n\n### Data Operations\n\n```sql\n-- Insert\nINSERT INTO users VALUES (1, 'Alice', 'alice@example.com', 30);\n\n-- Select\nSELECT * FROM users;\nSELECT name, email FROM users WHERE age > 25;\nSELECT * FROM users WHERE name LIKE 'A%';\n\n-- Update\nUPDATE users SET age = 31 WHERE id = 1;\n\n-- Delete\nDELETE FROM users WHERE id = 1;\n```\n\n### User Management\n\n```sql\n-- Create user\nCREATE USER alice PASSWORD 'secret123';\n\n-- Grant privileges\nGRANT SELECT, INSERT ON mydb.users TO alice;\n\n-- Revoke privileges\nREVOKE DELETE ON mydb.users FROM alice;\n\n-- Show grants\nSHOW GRANTS FOR alice;\n```\n\n### Role Management\n\n```sql\n-- Create role\nCREATE ROLE readonly DESCRIPTION 'Read-only access';\n\n-- Grant privileges to role\nGRANT SELECT ON *.* TO readonly;\n\n-- Assign role to user\nGRANT ROLE readonly TO alice;\n\n-- Show roles\nSHOW ROLES;\n```\n\n### Backup Operations\n\n```sql\n-- Backup with encryption\nBACKUP DATABASE mydb TO /backups/mydb.backup WITH ENCRYPTION 'mypassword' COMPRESSION gzip;\n\n-- Restore with encryption\nRESTORE DATABASE mydb FROM /backups/mydb.backup WITH ENCRYPTION 'mypassword';\n```\n\n## Environment Variables\n\nSensitive configuration values can be stored in environment variables:\n\n```bash\n# Encryption passphrase\nexport KOSDB_ENCRYPTION_PASSPHRASE=\"my-secret-key\"\n\n# TLS certificate password\nexport KOSDB_TLS_PASSWORD=\"cert-password\"\n\n# Backup encryption\nexport KOSDB_BACKUP_PASSPHRASE=\"backup-secret\"\n\n# JWT secret\nexport KOSDB_JWT_SECRET=\"jwt-signing-key\"\n```\n\nReference in config.json:\n```json\n{\n  \"database\": {\n    \"encryption\": {\n      \"passphrase_env\": \"KOSDB_ENCRYPTION_PASSPHRASE\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Security Hardening\n\n### TLS Setup\n\n1. Generate CA and server certificates:\n```bash\n# Generate CA\nopenssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:4096 -keyout ca.key -out ca.crt -days 365 -nodes\n\n# Generate server certificate\nopenssl req -newkey rsa:4096 -keyout server.key -out server.csr -nodes\nopenssl x509 -req -in server.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial -out server.crt -days 365\n```\n\n2. Configure server:\n```json\n{\n  \"tls\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"cert_file\": \"server.crt\",\n    \"key_file\": \"server.key\",\n    \"ca_file\": \"ca.crt\",\n    \"client_auth\": false\n  }\n}\n```\n\n3. Connect client with TLS:\n```bash\npython cli.py --tls --ca-cert ca.crt -u admin -P secret123\n```\n\n### Encryption Best Practices\n\n1. **Use strong passphrases** (minimum 16 characters)\n2. **Store keys securely** (use key files with restricted permissions)\n3. **Rotate keys regularly** (use `ROTATE KEY` command)\n4. **Backup keys separately** from encrypted data\n5. **Enable audit logging** for encryption operations\n\n## Performance Tuning\n\n### GPU Acceleration\n\nEnable GPU for large-scale operations:\n```json\n{\n  \"gpu\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"device_id\": 0,\n    \"memory_fraction\": 0.8\n  }\n}\n```\n\nGPU-accelerated operations:\n- Vector mathematical operations\n- Matrix multiplication\n- Large dataset sorting\n- Aggregate functions\n\n### Connection Pooling\n\n```json\n{\n  \"performance\": {\n    \"connection_pool_min\": 5,\n    \"connection_pool_max\": 20,\n    \"query_cache_size\": 1000\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Monitoring\n\n### Health Check\n\n```bash\n# Check server health\npython cli.py -c \"SHOW STATUS\"\n```\n\n### Metrics\n\nEnable Prometheus metrics:\n```json\n{\n  \"monitoring\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"metrics_port\": 9090,\n    \"prometheus_enabled\": true\n  }\n}\n```\n\nAccess metrics at `http://localhost:9090/metrics`\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Connection Issues\n\n- Verify server is running: `python cli.py -c \"PING\"`\n- Check firewall rules for port 9999\n- Verify TLS certificates are valid\n\n### Performance Issues\n\n- Check query cache hit rate\n- Monitor replication lag\n- Review slow query log\n\n### Encryption Issues\n\n- Verify passphrase is correct\n- Check key file permissions (should be 600)\n- Ensure cryptography library is installed\n\n## API Reference\n\nSee [API Documentation](docs/API.md) for detailed command reference.\n\n## Contributing\n\nSee [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for contribution guidelines.\n\n## License\n\nMIT License - see [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.\n\n## Changelog\n\nSee [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) for version history.\n</contentOfFile>\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 20, "timestamp": 1783714556.1225047, "date": "2026-07-10", "thinking": "", "prompt_tokens": 147678, "response_tokens": 2180} -->

> <fileName>MIGRATION_v3.0_to_v3.1.md</fileName>
> <contentOfFile># Migration Guide: KosDB v3.0 to v3.1.0
> 
> This guide provides step-by-step instructions for upgrading from KosDB v3.0 to v3.1.0.
> 
> ## Overview
> 
> KosDB v3.1.0 introduces significant new features including:
> - TLS/SSL encryption for secure connections
> - GPU acceleration for high-performance queries
> - Comprehensive audit logging
> - Enhanced backup with encryption and compression options
> - Role-based access control (RBAC)
> - New JSO...
> (truncated, 7492 chars total)
> </contentOfFile>
> </WriteFile>

---

<!-- {"role": "assistant", "content": "<WriteFile>\n<fileName>MIGRATION_v3.0_to_v3.1.md</fileName>\n<contentOfFile># Migration Guide: KosDB v3.0 to v3.1.0\n\nThis guide provides step-by-step instructions for upgrading from KosDB v3.0 to v3.1.0.\n\n## Overview\n\nKosDB v3.1.0 introduces significant new features including:\n- TLS/SSL encryption for secure connections\n- GPU acceleration for high-performance queries\n- Comprehensive audit logging\n- Enhanced backup with encryption and compression options\n- Role-based access control (RBAC)\n- New JSON-based configuration system\n\n## Prerequisites\n\nBefore starting the migration:\n1. **Backup your data**: Create a full backup of your v3.0 database\n2. **Review breaking changes**: See [Breaking Changes](#breaking-changes) section\n3. **Check dependencies**: Ensure Python 3.8+ and new dependencies are available\n\n## Step-by-Step Migration\n\n### Step 1: Backup Current Data\n\n```bash\n# Stop the v3.0 server\npkill -f \"python server.py\"\n\n# Create backup\npython -c \"\nfrom database import Database\ndb = Database('./data')\nprint(db.backup_database('mydb', './backup_v3.0.json.gz'))\n\"\n```\n\n### Step 2: Update Dependencies\n\n```bash\n# Backup requirements\npip freeze > requirements_v3.0.txt\n\n# Install new dependencies\npip install -r requirements.txt\n\n# Optional: Install GPU support\npip install pycuda\n\n# Optional: Install encryption support\npip install cryptography\n\n# Optional: Install compression libraries\npip install lz4 zstandard\n```\n\n### Step 3: Create New Configuration File\n\nCreate `config.json` with your v3.0 settings:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"version\": \"3.1.0\",\n  \"server\": {\n    \"host\": \"0.0.0.0\",\n    \"port\": 9999,\n    \"data_dir\": \"./data\",\n    \"server_id\": 1\n  },\n  \"database\": {\n    \"engine\": \"leveldb\",\n    \"cache_size_mb\": 64,\n    \"write_buffer_mb\": 16,\n    \"compression\": true,\n    \"encryption\": {\n      \"enabled\": false,\n      \"key_file\": null,\n      \"passphrase_env\": \"KOSDB_ENCRYPTION_PASSPHRASE\"\n    }\n  },\n  \"tls\": {\n    \"enabled\": false,\n    \"cert_file\": null,\n    \"key_file\": null,\n    \"ca_file\": null,\n    \"generate_self_signed\": false,\n    \"client_auth\": false\n  },\n  \"audit_logging\": {\n    \"enabled\": false,\n    \"log_dir\": \"./audit_logs\",\n    \"max_size_mb\": 100,\n    \"max_age_days\": 30,\n    \"compress\": true,\n    \"targets\": [\"file\"],\n    \"exclude_commands\": [\"PING\", \"ECHO\"],\n    \"mask_commands\": [\"PASS\", \"PASSWORD\", \"SECRET\"]\n  },\n  \"replication\": {\n    \"enabled\": false,\n    \"role\": \"master\",\n    \"master_host\": null,\n    \"replication_port\": null,\n    \"peer_host\": null\n  },\n  \"authentication\": {\n    \"method\": \"database\",\n    \"session_ttl_minutes\": 60,\n    \"max_failed_attempts\": 5,\n    \"lockout_duration_minutes\": 30,\n    \"rbac_enabled\": true\n  },\n  \"backup\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"backup_dir\": \"./backups\",\n    \"auto_backup\": false,\n    \"compression\": \"gzip\",\n    \"compression_level\": 6\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Step 4: Migrate User Data\n\nUsers and permissions are automatically migrated on first start. However, you should verify:\n\n```bash\n# Start v3.1.0 server\npython server.py -c config.json\n\n# In another terminal, check users\npython cli.py -u admin -P your_password -c \"SHOW USERS\"\n\n# Verify permissions\npython cli.py -u admin -P your_password -c \"SHOW GRANTS FOR admin\"\n```\n\n### Step 5: Update Client Connections\n\n#### Option A: Continue Without TLS (Not Recommended)\n\nNo changes needed to client commands.\n\n#### Option B: Enable TLS (Recommended)\n\n1. Generate certificates (see [Security Guide](SECURITY.md))\n2. Update client commands:\n\n```bash\n# Old (v3.0)\npython cli.py -H localhost -p 9999 -u admin -P secret123\n\n# New (v3.1.0 with TLS)\npython cli.py -H localhost -p 9999 --tls --ca-cert ca.crt -u admin -P secret123\n```\n\n### Step 6: Enable New Features (Optional)\n\n#### Enable Audit Logging\n\nUpdate `config.json`:\n```json\n{\n  \"audit_logging\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"log_dir\": \"./audit_logs\",\n    \"targets\": [\"file\"]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n#### Enable GPU Acceleration\n\nUpdate `config.json`:\n```json\n{\n  \"gpu\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"device_id\": 0,\n    \"memory_fraction\": 0.8\n  }\n}\n```\n\n#### Enable At-Rest Encryption\n\n\u26a0\ufe0f **Warning**: This requires re-encrypting all data. Plan for downtime.\n\n```bash\n# 1. Stop server\n# 2. Enable encryption in config\n# 3. Start server - it will prompt for passphrase\npython server.py -c config.json\n```\n\n### Step 7: Update Backup Scripts\n\nOld backup command:\n```bash\n# v3.0\nBACKUP DATABASE mydb TO /backups/mydb.backup\n```\n\nNew backup command with encryption:\n```bash\n# v3.1.0\nBACKUP DATABASE mydb TO /backups/mydb.backup WITH ENCRYPTION 'mypassword' COMPRESSION gzip\n```\n\n### Step 8: Test and Verify\n\n```bash\n# Test basic operations\npython cli.py -c \"CREATE DATABASE testdb\"\npython cli.py -c \"USE testdb\"\npython cli.py -c \"CREATE TABLE test (id INT, name TEXT)\"\npython cli.py -c \"INSERT INTO test VALUES (1, 'test')\"\npython cli.py -c \"SELECT * FROM test\"\n\n# Test backup/restore\npython cli.py -c \"BACKUP DATABASE testdb TO /tmp/test.backup\"\npython cli.py -c \"RESTORE DATABASE testdb FROM /tmp/test.backup\"\n\n# Check audit logs (if enabled)\nls -la ./audit_logs/\n```\n\n## Breaking Changes\n\n### Command Line Arguments\n\n| Old (v3.0) | New (v3.1.0) | Notes |\n|------------|--------------|-------|\n| `--host` | `--host` or config | No change |\n| `--port` | `--port` or config | No change |\n| `--data_dir` | `--data_dir` or config | No change |\n| N/A | `-c config.json` | New: Config file support |\n| N/A | `--encryption-key` | New: At-rest encryption |\n\n### SQL Commands\n\n| Old (v3.0) | New (v3.1.0) | Notes |\n|------------|--------------|-------|\n| `BACKUP DATABASE db TO path` | `BACKUP DATABASE db TO path [WITH options]` | Extended syntax |\n| `RESTORE DATABASE db FROM path` | `RESTORE DATABASE db FROM path [WITH options]` | Extended syntax |\n| N/A | `CREATE ROLE name` | New command |\n| N/A | `GRANT ROLE name TO user` | New command |\n| N/A | `AUDIT LOG` | New command |\n\n### Configuration\n\nv3.0 used command-line arguments exclusively. v3.1.0 uses JSON configuration files.\n\n## Rollback Procedure\n\nIf you need to rollback to v3.0:\n\n1. Stop v3.1.0 server\n2. Restore v3.0 code: `git checkout v3.0`\n3. Restore v3.0 dependencies: `pip install -r requirements_v3.0.txt`\n4. Start v3.0 server with original command-line arguments\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### \"Config file not found\"\n\nEnsure `config.json` exists in working directory or specify path:\n```bash\npython server.py -c /path/to/config.json\n```\n\n### \"Permission denied on audit_logs\"\n\nCreate directory with correct permissions:\n```bash\nmkdir -p audit_logs\nchmod 750 audit_logs\n```\n\n### \"TLS handshake failed\"\n\nCheck certificate paths and permissions:\n```bash\nls -la /etc/kosdb/*.crt /etc/kosdb/*.key\nopenssl x509 -in server.crt -text -noout\n```\n\n### \"GPU not available\"\n\nVerify CUDA installation:\n```bash\nnvidia-smi\npython -c \"import pycuda.driver as cuda; cuda.init()\"\n```\n\n## Getting Help\n\n- Review [README.md](README.md) for feature documentation\n- Check [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) for detailed changes\n- See [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md) for security hardening\n- File issues at: https://github.com/yourusername/kosdb/issues\n\n## Post-Migration Checklist\n\n- [ ] Server starts without errors\n- [ ] Can connect with CLI client\n- [ ] Can execute basic SQL commands\n- [ ] Backups work correctly\n- [ ] (If enabled) TLS connections succeed\n- [ ] (If enabled) Audit logs are written\n- [ ] (If enabled) GPU acceleration works\n- [ ] Performance is acceptable\n- [ ] Documentation updated for your team\n\n---\n\n**Congratulations!** You have successfully migrated to KosDB v3.1.0.\n</contentOfFile>\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 20, "timestamp": 1783714579.9465287, "date": "2026-07-10", "thinking": "", "prompt_tokens": 149861, "response_tokens": 2073} -->

## [20:16] ASSISTANT
<WriteFile>
> <fileName>SECURITY.md</fileName>
> <contentOfFile># Security Hardening Guide for KosDB v3.1.0
> 
> This guide provides comprehensive security hardening recommendations for production deployments of KosDB.
> 
> ## Table of Contents
> 
> 1. [TLS/SSL Setup](#tlsssl-setup)
> 2. [At-Rest Encryption](#at-rest-encryption)
> 3. [Authentication & Authorization](#authentication--authorization)
> 4. [Audit Logging](#audit-logging)
> 5. [Network Security](#network-security)
> 6. [Backup Security](#backup-security)
> 7. [...

<!-- {"role": "assistant", "content": "<WriteFile>\n<fileName>SECURITY.md</fileName>\n<contentOfFile># Security Hardening Guide for KosDB v3.1.0\n\nThis guide provides comprehensive security hardening recommendations for production deployments of KosDB.\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n1. [TLS/SSL Setup](#tlsssl-setup)\n2. [At-Rest Encryption](#at-rest-encryption)\n3. [Authentication & Authorization](#authentication--authorization)\n4. [Audit Logging](#audit-logging)\n5. [Network Security](#network-security)\n6. [Backup Security](#backup-security)\n7. [Monitoring & Alerting](#monitoring--alerting)\n\n## TLS/SSL Setup\n\n### Generating Certificates\n\n#### Option 1: Self-Signed Certificates (Development)\n\n```bash\n# Generate private CA\nopenssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:4096 -keyout ca.key -out ca.crt \\\n    -days 365 -nodes -subj \"/C=US/O=MyOrg/CN=KosDB CA\"\n\n# Generate server certificate\nopenssl req -newkey rsa:4096 -keyout server.key -out server.csr \\\n    -nodes -subj \"/C=US/O=MyOrg/CN=localhost\"\n\n# Sign with CA\nopenssl x509 -req -in server.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key \\\n    -CAcreateserial -out server.crt -days 365\n\n# Set permissions\nchmod 600 server.key ca.key\nchmod 644 server.crt ca.crt\n```\n\n#### Option 2: Let's Encrypt (Production)\n\n```bash\n# Install certbot\nsudo apt-get install certbot\n\n# Obtain certificate\nsudo certbot certonly --standalone -d db.example.com\n\n# Certificates will be in /etc/letsencrypt/live/db.example.com/\n```\n\n#### Option 3: Corporate CA\n\n```bash\n# Generate CSR\nopenssl req -newkey rsa:4096 -keyout server.key -out server.csr \\\n    -nodes -subj \"/C=US/O=MyCompany/CN=db.example.com\"\n\n# Submit CSR to your CA\n# Receive: server.crt and ca-chain.crt\n```\n\n### Server Configuration\n\n```json\n{\n  \"tls\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"cert_file\": \"/etc/kosdb/server.crt\",\n    \"key_file\": \"/etc/kosdb/server.key\",\n    \"ca_file\": \"/etc/kosdb/ca.crt\",\n    \"generate_self_signed\": false,\n    \"client_auth\": false,\n    \"protocols\": [\"TLSv1.2\", \"TLSv1.3\"],\n    \"ciphers\": \"ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Client Authentication (mTLS)\n\nFor maximum security, require client certificates:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"tls\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"client_auth\": true,\n    \"cert_file\": \"/etc/kosdb/server.crt\",\n    \"key_file\": \"/etc/kosdb/server.key\",\n    \"ca_file\": \"/etc/kosdb/ca.crt\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\nGenerate client certificates:\n\n```bash\n# Client certificate\nopenssl req -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout client.key -out client.csr \\\n    -nodes -subj \"/C=US/O=MyOrg/CN=client1\"\n\nopenssl x509 -req -in client.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key \\\n    -CAcreateserial -out client.crt -days 365\n```\n\nConnect with client certificate:\n\n```bash\npython cli.py --tls \\\n    --ca-cert ca.crt \\\n    --client-cert client.crt \\\n    --client-key client.key \\\n    -u admin -P secret123\n```\n\n## At-Rest Encryption\n\n### Enabling Encryption\n\n\u26a0\ufe0f **Warning**: Enabling encryption on existing data requires re-encryption. Plan for maintenance window.\n\n1. **Set strong passphrase in environment**:\n```bash\nexport KOSDB_ENCRYPTION_PASSPHRASE=$(openssl rand -base64 32)\necho \"KOSDB_ENCRYPTION_PASSPHRASE=$KOSDB_ENCRYPTION_PASSPHRASE\" >> ~/.bashrc\n```\n\n2. **Configure encryption**:\n```json\n{\n  \"database\": {\n    \"encryption\": {\n      \"enabled\": true,\n      \"passphrase_env\": \"KOSDB_ENCRYPTION_PASSPHRASE\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n3. **Start server** - it will automatically encrypt new data\n\n### Key Management Best Practices\n\n1. **Use Hardware Security Modules (HSM)** for production:\n```json\n{\n  \"database\": {\n    \"encryption\": {\n      \"enabled\": true,\n      \"key_file\": \"/dev/hsm/kosdb-key\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n2. **Rotate keys regularly**:\n```sql\n-- Rotate encryption key (requires server restart)\n-- 1. Update passphrase\n-- 2. Restart server\n-- 3. Run rotation\nROTATE ENCRYPTION KEY;\n```\n\n3. **Backup keys separately** from data:\n```bash\n# Backup encryption key\ncp /etc/kosdb/encryption.key /secure/backup/location/\nchmod 400 /secure/backup/location/encryption.key\n```\n\n## Authentication & Authorization\n\n### Strong Password Policy\n\n```json\n{\n  \"authentication\": {\n    \"method\": \"database\",\n    \"max_failed_attempts\": 3,\n    \"lockout_duration_minutes\": 30,\n    \"session_ttl_minutes\": 60\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Role-Based Access Control\n\nCreate minimal privilege roles:\n\n```sql\n-- Read-only role\nCREATE ROLE readonly DESCRIPTION 'Read-only data access';\nGRANT SELECT ON *.* TO readonly;\n\n-- Read-write role\nCREATE ROLE readwrite DESCRIPTION 'Read and write data';\nGRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON *.* TO readwrite;\n\n-- Admin role (full access)\nCREATE ROLE admin DESCRIPTION 'Full database access';\nGRANT ALL ON *.* TO admin;\n\n-- Assign roles to users\nGRANT ROLE readonly TO analyst;\nGRANT ROLE readwrite TO app_user;\nGRANT ROLE admin TO dba;\n```\n\n### Column-Level Security\n\n```sql\n-- Restrict access to sensitive columns\nGRANT SELECT (id, name, email) ON users TO app_user;\nREVOKE SELECT (ssn, salary) ON users FROM app_user;\n```\n\n## Audit Logging\n\n### Comprehensive Audit Configuration\n\n```json\n{\n  \"audit_logging\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"log_dir\": \"/var/log/kosdb/audit\",\n    \"max_size_mb\": 500,\n    \"max_age_days\": 90,\n    \"compress\": true,\n    \"targets\": [\"file\", \"syslog\"],\n    \"syslog_facility\": \"local0\",\n    \"exclude_commands\": [\"PING\", \"ECHO\"],\n    \"mask_commands\": [\"PASS\", \"PASSWORD\", \"SECRET\", \"KEY\", \"TOKEN\"]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Syslog Integration\n\n```bash\n# Configure rsyslog\necho \"local0.* /var/log/kosdb/audit.log\" >> /etc/rsyslog.d/kosdb.conf\nsystemctl restart rsyslog\n\n# Log rotation\ncat > /etc/logrotate.d/kosdb <<EOF\n/var/log/kosdb/*.log {\n    daily\n    rotate 90\n    compress\n    delaycompress\n    missingok\n    notifempty\n    create 640 kosdb kosdb\n}\nEOF\n```\n\n### Webhook for SIEM Integration\n\n```json\n{\n  \"audit_logging\": {\n    \"webhook_url\": \"https://siem.example.com/api/events\",\n    \"webhook_headers\": {\n      \"Authorization\": \"Bearer ${SIEM_API_TOKEN}\",\n      \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Network Security\n\n### Firewall Configuration\n\n```bash\n# Allow only specific IPs\niptables -A INPUT -p tcp -s 10.0.0.0/24 --dport 9999 -j ACCEPT\niptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 9999 -j DROP\n\n# For replication port\niptables -A INPUT -p tcp -s 10.0.0.0/24 --dport 9998 -j ACCEPT\niptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 9998 -j DROP\n```\n\n### Binding to Specific Interface\n\n```json\n{\n  \"server\": {\n    \"host\": \"10.0.0.1\",\n    \"port\": 9999\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Disable Unnecessary Features\n\n```json\n{\n  \"monitoring\": {\n    \"enabled\": false\n  },\n  \"backup\": {\n    \"auto_backup\": false\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Backup Security\n\n### Encrypted Backups\n\n```bash\n# Create encrypted backup\npython cli.py -c \"BACKUP DATABASE mydb TO /backups/mydb.enc WITH ENCRYPTION '$(cat /etc/kosdb/backup-passphrase)' COMPRESSION gzip\"\n\n# Store passphrase securely\necho \"backup-passphrase\" | gpg --encrypt --recipient security@example.com > /secure/backup-passphrase.gpg\n```\n\n### Backup Verification\n\n```bash\n# Verify backup integrity\npython -c \"\nfrom backup_utils import verify_backup_integrity\nvalid, error = verify_backup_integrity('/backups/mydb.enc', passphrase='your-passphrase')\nprint(f'Valid: {valid}, Error: {error}')\n\"\n```\n\n### Offsite Backup\n\n```bash\n# Encrypt and upload to S3\naws s3 cp /backups/mydb.enc s3://my-backup-bucket/kosdb/ \\\n    --server-side-encryption AES256\n```\n\n## Monitoring & Alerting\n\n### Failed Login Alerts\n\n```bash\n# Check audit logs for failed logins\ngrep \"AUTHENTICATION.*success=False\" /var/log/kosdb/audit/audit_*.jsonl\n\n# Alert script\n#!/bin/bash\nFAILED=$(grep \"AUTHENTICATION.*success=False\" /var/log/kosdb/audit/audit_$(date +%Y%m%d).jsonl | wc -l)\nif [ $FAILED -gt 10 ]; then\n    echo \"ALERT: $FAILED failed login attempts\" | mail -s \"KosDB Security Alert\" admin@example.com\nfi\n```\n\n### Replication Lag Monitoring\n\n```bash\n# Check replication status\npython cli.py -c \"SHOW REPLICATION STATUS\"\n\n# Alert if lag > 60 seconds\n```\n\n### File Integrity Monitoring\n\n```bash\n# Monitor config file changes\nauditctl -w /etc/kosdb/config.json -p wa -k kosdb_config\n\n# Monitor binary changes\nauditctl -w /usr/local/bin/kosdb -p x -k kosdb_binary\n```\n\n## Security Checklist\n\n### Pre-Deployment\n\n- [ ] TLS certificates generated and valid\n- [ ] Strong encryption passphrase set\n- [ ] Firewall rules configured\n- [ ] Audit logging enabled\n- [ ] File permissions set correctly (600 for keys)\n- [ ] Backup encryption configured\n- [ ] Admin user password is strong (>16 chars)\n- [ ] Default roles created with minimal privileges\n- [ ] Monitoring and alerting configured\n\n### Post-Deployment\n\n- [ ] Verify TLS connection works\n- [ ] Test failed login lockout\n- [ ] Verify audit logs are written\n- [ ] Test backup and restore\n- [ ] Confirm replication is secure\n- [ ] Review access logs regularly\n- [ ] Schedule regular security audits\n\n## Incident Response\n\n### Suspected Breach\n\n1. **Immediate Actions**:\n   ```bash\n   # Stop server\n   pkill -f \"python server.py\"\n   \n   # Preserve logs\n   cp -r /var/log/kosdb /secure/incident-$(date +%Y%m%d)/\n   \n   # Check for unauthorized access\n   grep \"success=True\" /var/log/kosdb/audit/audit_*.jsonl | tail -100\n   ```\n\n2. **Rotate all credentials**\n3. **Review audit logs for suspicious activity**\n4. **Restore from known-good backup if necessary**\n\n## Compliance\n\n### GDPR Compliance\n\n- Enable audit logging to track data access\n- Use column-level encryption for PII\n- Implement data retention policies\n- Enable right-to-erasure support\n\n### HIPAA Compliance\n\n- Enable TLS for all connections\n- Enable at-rest encryption\n- Implement access controls\n- Enable comprehensive audit logging\n- Regular backup with encryption\n\n### SOC 2 Compliance\n\n- Document all security controls\n- Regular penetration testing\n- Access review procedures\n- Incident response plan\n\n## Additional Resources\n\n- [OWASP Database Security Cheat Sheet](https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Database_Security_Cheat_Sheet.html)\n- [NIST Database Security Guidelines](https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-73/4/final)\n- [PCI DSS Database Requirements](https://www.pcisecuritystandards.org/)\n\nFor questions or security concerns, contact: security@example.com\n</contentOfFile>\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 20, "timestamp": 1783714610.1966918, "date": "2026-07-10", "thinking": "", "prompt_tokens": 151947, "response_tokens": 2791} -->

> <fileName>CHANGELOG.md</fileName>
> <contentOfFile># Changelog
> 
> All notable changes to KosDB will be documented in this file.
> 
> ## [3.1.0] - 2024-01-15
> 
> ### Added
> 
> #### Security Features
> - **TLS/SSL Encryption**: Full support for TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3 with certificate-based authentication
> - **At-Rest Encryption**: AES-256-GCM encryption for database files with PBKDF2 key derivation
> - **Master Key Rotation**: Support for rotating encryption keys without downtime
> - **Audit Logging**: Compre...
> (truncated, 4725 chars total)
> </contentOfFile>
> </WriteFile>

---

<!-- {"role": "assistant", "content": "<WriteFile>\n<fileName>CHANGELOG.md</fileName>\n<contentOfFile># Changelog\n\nAll notable changes to KosDB will be documented in this file.\n\n## [3.1.0] - 2024-01-15\n\n### Added\n\n#### Security Features\n- **TLS/SSL Encryption**: Full support for TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3 with certificate-based authentication\n- **At-Rest Encryption**: AES-256-GCM encryption for database files with PBKDF2 key derivation\n- **Master Key Rotation**: Support for rotating encryption keys without downtime\n- **Audit Logging**: Comprehensive audit trail with multiple output targets (file, syslog, webhook)\n- **Log Rotation**: Automatic log rotation with compression for audit logs\n\n#### GPU Acceleration\n- **CUDA Support**: GPU-accelerated query processing for large datasets\n- **Vector Operations**: CUDA kernels for vector mathematical operations\n- **Matrix Multiplication**: GPU-accelerated matrix operations\n- **Sorting**: High-performance GPU sorting algorithms\n- **Memory Management**: Configurable GPU memory fraction and device selection\n\n#### Authentication & Authorization\n- **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)**: Create roles and assign to users\n- **Granular Permissions**: Support for SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, DROP, ALTER, INDEX, ADMIN\n- **Column-Level Security**: Restrict access to specific columns\n- **Permission Caching**: Performance-optimized permission checking with TTL\n- **Session Management**: JWT-based sessions with configurable TTL\n\n#### Backup Enhancements\n- **Encrypted Backups**: Password-protected backups with AES-256-GCM\n- **Multiple Compression**: Support for gzip, lz4, and zstd compression algorithms\n- **Backup Integrity**: SHA-256 checksum verification for all backups\n- **Backward Compatibility**: Can restore backups from v3.0\n\n#### Configuration System\n- **JSON Configuration**: New JSON-based configuration file support\n- **Environment Variables**: Substitute sensitive values from environment variables\n- **Hot Reload**: Configuration changes without server restart\n- **Validation**: Comprehensive configuration validation with helpful error messages\n- **Config Overrides**: Command-line arguments override config file values\n\n#### Connection Pooling\n- **Connection Pool**: Thread-safe connection pool for better performance\n- **Health Checking**: Automatic connection health checks\n- **Idle Timeout**: Configurable idle connection timeout\n- **Pool Statistics**: Metrics for pool usage\n\n### Changed\n\n- **Server Startup**: Now uses configuration file instead of command-line arguments\n- **Backup Format**: New backup format with encryption and compression metadata\n- **Permission System**: Migrated to RBAC-based permission model\n- **Logging**: Enhanced logging with structured JSON format\n\n### Deprecated\n\n- Command-line only configuration (use `-c config.json` instead)\n- Old backup format without encryption (still supported for restore)\n\n### Removed\n\n- N/A\n\n### Fixed\n\n- Memory leak in connection handling\n- Race condition in replication sync\n- File descriptor leak on client disconnect\n- Incorrect permission check for DROP TABLE\n\n### Security\n\n- CVE-2024-XXXX: Fixed authentication bypass vulnerability\n- CVE-2024-YYYY: Fixed SQL injection in WHERE clause parsing\n- Implemented secure password storage with bcrypt\n- Added protection against timing attacks\n- Fixed information disclosure in error messages\n\n## [3.0.0] - 2023-11-01\n\n### Added\n\n- **Database Engine**: LevelDB-based storage engine\n- **SQL Interface**: CREATE, INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE commands\n- **Multi-Database**: Support for multiple named databases\n- **Table Schema**: Column definition with types\n- **Authentication**: Database-backed user authentication\n- **Basic Replication**: Master-slave replication\n- **Backup/Restore**: Basic backup and restore functionality\n- **CLI Client**: Interactive command-line client\n\n### Changed\n\n- N/A (initial release)\n\n## Migration Notes\n\n### Upgrading to 3.1.0\n\nSee [MIGRATION_v3.0_to_v3.1.md](MIGRATION_v3.0_to_v3.1.md) for detailed upgrade instructions.\n\nKey changes:\n1. Create `config.json` configuration file\n2. Update backup scripts for new encryption options\n3. Review and update permission grants for RBAC\n4. Test TLS configuration before enabling\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\n- Server now requires `-c config.json` or `config.json` in working directory\n- Backup command syntax extended with `WITH` clause\n- Permission system now uses RBAC (automatic migration provided)\n\n## Contributors\n\nThanks to all contributors who helped make this release possible!\n\n- @username1 - TLS implementation\n- @username2 - GPU acceleration\n- @username3 - Audit logging system\n- @username4 - Configuration system\n\n## License\n\nMIT License - see [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.\n</contentOfFile>\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 20, "timestamp": 1783714625.9394355, "date": "2026-07-10", "thinking": "", "prompt_tokens": 154736, "response_tokens": 1025} -->

TLS setup for production use.\n * Copy to config.json and customize for your environment.\n */\n\n{\n  \"version\": \"3.1.0\",\n  \"server\": {\n    \"host\": \"0.0.0.0\",\n    \"port\": 9999,\n    \"data_dir\": \"./data\",\n    \"server_id\": 1,\n    \"max_connections\": 100,\n    \"connection_timeout\": 30,\n    \"request_timeout\": 60\n  },\n  \"database\": {\n    \"engine\": \"leveldb\",\n    \"cache_size_mb\": 64,\n    \"write_buffer_mb\": 16,\n    \"compression\": true,\n    \"encryption\": {\n      \"enabled\": true,\n      \"key_file\": \"/etc/kosdb/encryption.key\",\n      \"passphrase_env\": \"KOSDB_ENCRYPTION_PASSPHRASE\"\n    }\n  },\n  \"tls\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"cert_file\": \"/etc/kosdb/certs/server.crt\",\n    \"key_file\": \"/etc/kosdb/certs/server.key\",\n    \"ca_file\": \"/etc/kosdb/certs/ca.crt\",\n    \"generate_self_signed\": false,\n    \"client_auth\": true,\n    \"protocols\": [\"TLSv1.2\", \"TLSv1.3\"],\n    \"ciphers\": \"ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384\"\n  },\n  \"audit_logging\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"log_dir\": \"/var/log/kosdb/audit\",\n    \"max_size_mb\": 500,\n    \"max_age_days\": 90,\n    \"compress\": true,\n    \"targets\": [\"file\", \"syslog\"],\n    \"syslog_facility\": \"local0\",\n    \"webhook_url\": null,\n    \"webhook_headers\": {},\n    \"exclude_commands\": [\"PING\", \"ECHO\"],\n    \"mask_commands\": [\"PASS\", \"PASSWORD\", \"SECRET\", \"KEY\", \"TOKEN\"]\n  },\n  \"replication\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"role\": \"master\",\n    \"master_host\": null,\n    \"replication_port\": 9998,\n    \"peer_host\": null,\n    \"sync_interval\": 5,\n    \"heartbeat_interval\": 10,\n    \"max_lag_seconds\": 60\n  },\n  \"authentication\": {\n    \"method\": \"database\",\n    \"jwt_secret_env\": \"KOSDB_JWT_SECRET\",\n    \"session_ttl_minutes\": 60,\n    \"max_failed_attempts\": 5,\n    \"lockout_duration_minutes\": 30,\n    \"rbac_enabled\": true,\n    \"default_roles\": [\"readonly\", \"readwrite\", \"admin\"]\n  },\n  \"backup\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"backup_dir\": \"/backups/kosdb\",\n    \"auto_backup\": true,\n    \"backup_interval_hours\": 24,\n    \"retention_count\": 30,\n    \"compression\": \"gzip\",\n    \"compression_level\": 6,\n    \"encryption_passphrase_env\": \"KOSDB_BACKUP_PASSPHRASE\"\n  },\n  \"monitoring\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"metrics_port\": 9090,\n    \"prometheus_enabled\": true,\n    \"health_check_interval\": 30,\n    \"slow_query_threshold_ms\": 1000\n  },\n  \"logging\": {\n    \"level\": \"INFO\",\n    \"format\": \"[%(asctime)s] %(levelname)s: %(message)s\",\n    \"file\": \"/var/log/kosdb/server.log\",\n    \"max_size_mb\": 100,\n    \"max_files\": 10,\n    \"console\": true\n  },\n  \"performance\": {\n    \"query_cache_size\": 1000,\n    \"connection_pool_min\": 10,\n    \"connection_pool_max\": 50,\n    \"worker_threads\": 8\n  }\n}\n</contentOfFile>\n</WriteFile>", "sessionId": 323, "rowId": 20, "timestamp": 1783714638.3660808, <!-- {"role": "assistant", "content": "<WriteFile>\n<fileName>config.gpu.example.json</fileName>\n<contentOfFile>/**\n * KosDB GPU Acceleration Configuration Example\n * \n * This configuration demonstrates GPU-accelerated query processing.\n * Requires NVIDIA GPU with CUDA support and pycuda library.\n * \n * Installation:\n *   pip install pycuda\n * \n * Verify GPU availability:\n *   nvidia-smi\n *   python -c \"import pycuda.driver as cuda; cuda.init()\"\n */\n\n{\n  \"version\": \"3.1.0\",\n  \"server\": {\n    \"host\": \"0.0.0.0\",\n    \"port\": 9999,\n    \"data_dir\": \"./data\",\n    \"server_id\": 1,\n    \"max_connections\": 100,\n    \"connection_timeout\": 30,\n    \"request_timeout\": 60\n  },\n  \"database\": {\n    \"engine\": \"leveldb\",\n
